item.text: I’ll review the design against the programme gates, feasibility evidence, relevant literature, and repository workflow context. I’ll first load the Beads instructions and identify the exact supporting documents, then give a maintainer-facing verdict without changing files.

item.command: /bin/bash -lc 'bd prime && rg --files docs/next | sort && git status --short --branch'

item.aggregated_output:
[bd prime] If this output is truncated by your host, read the full persisted hook output before continuing; it may contain project memories and session rules not visible in the preview.

# Beads Workflow Context

> **Context Recovery**: Run `bd prime` after compaction, clear, or new session
> Hooks auto-call this in Claude Code when a beads workspace is resolved

# 🚨 SESSION CLOSE PROTOCOL 🚨

**CRITICAL**: Before saying "done" or "complete", you MUST run this checklist:

```
[ ] 1. git status              (check what changed)
[ ] 2. git add <files>         (stage code changes)
[ ] 3. git commit -m "..."     (commit code)
[ ] 4. git push                (push to remote)
```

**NEVER skip this.** Work is not done until pushed.

## Core Rules
- **Default**: Use beads for ALL task tracking (`bd create`, `bd ready`, `bd close`)
- **Prohibited**: Do NOT use TodoWrite, TaskCreate, or markdown files for task tracking
- **Workflow**: Create beads issue BEFORE writing code, mark in_progress when starting
- **Memory**: Use `bd remember "insight"` for persistent knowledge across sessions. Do NOT use MEMORY.md files — they fragment across accounts. Search with `bd memories <keyword>`.
- Persistence you don't need beats lost context
- Git workflow: beads auto-commit to Dolt, run `git push` at session end
- Session management: check `bd ready` for available work

## Essential Commands

### Finding Work
- `bd ready` - Show issues ready to work (no blockers)
- `bd list --status=open` - All open issues
- `bd list --status=in_progress` - Your active work
- `bd show <id>` - Detailed issue view with dependencies

### Creating & Updating
- `bd create --title="Summary of this issue" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task|bug|feature --priority=2` - New issue
  - Priority: 0-4 or P0-P4 (0=critical, 2=medium, 4=backlog). NOT "high"/"medium"/"low"
- `bd update <id> --claim` - Claim work
- `bd update <id> --assignee=username` - Assign to someone
- `bd update <id> --title/--description/--notes/--design` - Update fields inline
- `bd close <id>` - Mark complete
- `bd close <id1> <id2> ...` - Close multiple issues at once (more efficient)
- `bd close <id> --reason="explanation"` - Close with reason
- **Tip**: When creating multiple issues/tasks/epics, use parallel subagents for efficiency
- **WARNING**: Do NOT use `bd edit` - it opens $EDITOR (vim/nano) which blocks agents

### Dependencies & Blocking
- `bd dep add <issue> <depends-on>` - Add dependency (issue depends on depends-on)
- `bd blocked` - Show all blocked issues
- `bd show <id>` - See what's blocking/blocked by this issue

### Sync & Collaboration
- `bd dolt push` - Push beads to Dolt remote
- `bd dolt pull` - Pull beads from Dolt remote
- `bd search <query>` - Search issues by keyword

### Project Health
- `bd stats` - Project statistics (open/closed/blocked counts)
- `bd doctor` - Check for issues (sync problems, missing hooks)
- `bd doctor --check=conventions` - Check for convention drift (lint, stale, orphans)

### Quality Tools
- `bd create --validate` - Check description has required sections
- `bd create --acceptance="criteria"` - Set acceptance criteria (checked by --validate)
- `bd create --design="decisions"` - Record design decisions
- `bd create --notes="context"` - Add supplementary notes
- `bd config set validation.on-create warn` - Auto-validate on every create
- `bd lint` - Check existing issues for missing sections

### Lifecycle & Hygiene
- `bd defer <id> --until="date"` - Defer work to a future date
- `bd supersede <id> --with=<new-id>` - Mark issue as superseded
- `bd close <id> --suggest-next` - Show newly unblocked issues after closing
- `bd stale` - Find issues with no recent activity
- `bd orphans` - Find issues with broken dependencies
- `bd preflight` - Pre-PR checks (lint, stale, orphans)
- `bd human <id>` - Flag for human decision (list/respond/dismiss)

### Structured Workflows
- `bd formula list` - See available workflow templates
- `bd mol pour <name>` - Start structured workflow from formula

## Common Workflows

**Starting work:**
```bash
bd ready           # Find available work
bd show <id>       # Review issue details
bd update <id> --claim  # Claim it
```

**Completing work:**
```bash
bd close <id1> <id2> ...    # Close all completed issues at once
git add . && git commit -m "..."  # Commit code changes
git push                    # Push to remote
```

**Creating dependent work:**
```bash
# Run bd create commands in parallel (use subagents for many items)
bd create --title="Implement feature X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=feature
bd create --title="Write tests for X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task
bd dep add beads-yyy beads-xxx  # Tests depend on Feature (Feature blocks tests)
```

docs/next/lit/TINY.md:609:# after both exist:

The pipeline below is the buildable artifact this bead specifies; P3-T-FRONT-IMPL
(§13) implements it.

```
S0 ENUMERATE   roster × transform lattice → static candidate list
               + compute packed bytes analytically → drop static-infeasible (LOGGED)
S1 SCREEN      dev mini-suite (pinned ~10% stratified subset of the dev split)
               → cheap dev-index proxy per candidate; successive halving keeps
               top half per family, ≥2 rounds
S2 OPTIMISE    survivors get metered tuning inside T_k (§3.3): decoding params,
               retrieval k / thresholds, TTC policy params, recovery training
               for pruned entries — FIXED selection rule (§3.2), all spend logged
S3 MEASURE     full dev-split run under KOT-SIZE/2 + KOT-COST/2 on the P3-D-HW
               rig, warm + cold, pinned concurrency + output-length discipline
S4 ADMIT       filter against every component of B_k → F(B_k) candidate set
S5 ENVELOPE    staircase + Pareto extraction over ALL measured points; dominated
               + inadmissible points reported; B_k marked
S6 PIN         freeze F(B_k): config/weights/index/verifier hashes, seeds,
               decoding params, the full builder log → content hash → the
               frontier version for that rung
```

### 3.1 Dev split (leakage discipline)

- DECISION: all builder selection runs on a **dev split disjoint from the frozen
  index suite AND from every sealed release by construction** (sealed items are
  produced post-freeze and never touch the builder; ASM-0812). The dev split is
  pinned by P3-D-INDEX alongside the suite; the builder never sees a frozen-suite
  or sealed item [STIPULATED: ASM-0812 executed; P3F-A2].

### 3.2 The fixed selection rule

- DECISION: per candidate family, select the config maximising the **dev-split
  estimate of KOT-AI-INDEX/2 restricted to that rung's above-floor components**
  (the floor/exclusion criterion registered from P3-E-CAL calibration data, per
  EVAL's correction — components may not be dropped post hoc), subject to
  admissibility; ties broken by lower measured cost vector (lexicographic:
  energy, then p95). Near-floor MC components are excluded from the SELECTION
  metric because their signal is noise near chance [LIT-BACKED: EVAL.md §5
  (Schaeffer arXiv:2304.15004, 2023)] — they remain in the frozen-suite
  MEASUREMENT of the final comparators [STIPULATED: ASM-0812 fixed-selection-rule
  clause executed; P3F-A2].

### 3.3 Total-tuning-compute symmetry (the T_k meter)

- DECISION: tuning symmetry binds on **total tuning compute actually spent**
  (accelerator-hours + CPU-hours, logged per arm into KOT-LIFE/1), NOT config
  counts (ASM-0812; data-matched ≠ compute-matched [LIT-BACKED: EVAL.md §7
  (BabyLM-2 findings arXiv:2412.05149, 2024)]). Mechanism: a per-rung cap **T_k applies per arm** — every comparator
  FAMILY (C1-quant, C2-pruned, C3-RAG, C4-TTC, C5-twin count as separate arms)
  and the candidate system S each spend ≤ T_k on search + adaptation; the meter
  is the builder's own job-level accounting, audit-trailed. Proposed values,
  maintainer-adjustable at KOT-FAIR/2 freeze: T_1 = 25 GPU-h/arm (R-1), T_2 =
  100 GPU-h/arm (R-2); R-0 arms run inside the free pool with the same logging
  [STIPULATED: ASM-0812 tuning-symmetry operationalised; P3F-A3-T]. An S that
  spends more than T_k has not won
  (ASM-0812 verbatim); a comparator arm that exhausts T_k freezes at its best
  logged config.
- Donor pretraining compute is INHERITED and declared on the lifecycle ledger
  (KOT-LIFE/1), never netted out and never counted against T_k — symmetric, since
  S's families also start from donors [STIPULATED: P3F-A3-T].

---

## 4. The adaptive test-time-compute recipe (resolving RAG.md's five OPEN items)

The lit-review verdict this section answers: "'implement Snell-style' is not yet
an executable baseline recipe" [LIT-BACKED: RAG.md §4/§7.6/§8]. The verified
envelope it must respect: the >4× and 14× findings are separate, conditional,
PaLM-2/MATH-scale results with the verifier's training compute off their ledger;
nothing is measured in our band or off math [LIT-BACKED: RAG.md §4 + EVAL.md §7].
Clause (iv) accordingly reads "mandatory to CONSIDER, include where
task-appropriate and budget-feasible" [LIT-BACKED: EVAL.md §9].

1. **Budgeted difficulty estimator [STIPULATED: P3F-A3].** Default estimator:
   **self-charging agreement escalation** — draw n₀ = 4 samples; if the majority
   answer's agreement ≥ a pinned threshold τ, stop and emit it; else escalate to
   the next stage of the policy ladder. The estimator IS the first stage of the
   policy, so its compute is charged to the arm's per-query cost by construction
   — there is no free oracle difficulty label. A trained router (small logistic
   head on sample-agreement + logprob features) is an OPTIONAL grid member, its
   training charged to T_k and its weights to bytes_max.
2. **Eligible policy ladder per budget rung [STIPULATED: P3F-A3].**

   | Stage | Policy | Enters the grid iff |
   |---|---|---|
   | P0 | greedy / single sample | always |
   | P1 | self-consistency@N, N ∈ {4, 16, 64} adaptive | per-query cost fits B_k latency/energy |
   | P2 | best-of-N + OUTCOME verifier (Cobbe-style) | verifier trains within T_k AND verifier bytes fit bytes_max |
   | P3 | PRM-weighted BoN / beam (Math-Shepherd-style auto-labels, no human-label budget) | as P2; PRM auto-labelling compute also inside T_k |

   Off-the-shelf open PRMs (e.g. the Skywork-PRM-1.5B class f2b already ran
   [MEASURED: registry/verdicts/f2b-replicate.json comparator]) are eligible
   ONLY where their bytes fit bytes_max — at the R-1 anchor budget
   (135M-class bytes) a 1.5B PRM is inadmissible by arithmetic; the R-1 P2/P3
   verifier is necessarily small and trained within T_k [STIPULATED: P3F-A3].
3. **Verifier build costs on the ledger [STIPULATED: P3F-A3].** Verifier/PRM
   training + auto-labelling compute → KOT-LIFE/1 (the ledger's cautionary-tale
   clause [LIT-BACKED: EVAL.md §7]); verifier weights → KOT-SIZE/2 figure (1);
   verifier inference → KOT-COST/2 per query. No component is free.
4. **Base-success ≈ 0 fallback [STIPULATED: P3F-A3].** If stage-1 agreement is
   below a pinned floor τ₀ (no two samples agree at n₀ AND max logprob below a
   pinned percentile), the policy falls back to P0 (emit best single sample or
   the benchmark's abstention token where the index scores abstention) and spends
   nothing further — encoding the measured edge that test-time compute does not
   rescue items the base model cannot touch [LIT-BACKED: RAG.md §4, Snell
   envelope].
5. **Non-math calibration gate [STIPULATED: P3F-A3].** Before any TTC arm
   enters a frozen F(B_k): a calibration measurement (co-scheduled with
   P3-E-CAL, same pinned harness) runs the policy ladder vs P0 on the DEV split
   per index DOMAIN. A TTC policy enters the frontier only in domains where its
   dev-split gain over P0 has LCB95 > 0 at that rung; elsewhere the arm freezes
   as P0. The Snell >4×/14× figures are never assumed to transfer — transfer to
   our band and suite is measured or the arm stays out
   [EXTRAPOLATION-barrier made explicit; LIT-BACKED: RAG.md §7.6].
6. **Large-budget decay reporting [STIPULATED: P3F-A3].** For every P2/P3 arm
   the builder publishes the full N-sweep curve (N ∈ {1, 4, 16, 64} within
   budget). If verifier-guided performance declines at high N (the scaling-flaws
   shape — shown for the tested verifiers, not universal [LIT-BACKED: RAG.md
   §4]), the frozen config takes the arg-max N (the selection rule already does
   this) and the decay curve is published beside the frontier. Symmetric honesty
   clause: the symbolic verifier's claimed immunity to this decay on its covered
   slice is a claim P3-E-VL-1 must measure, never a premise here [LIT-BACKED:
   RAG.md §5].

---

## 5. The matched-retrieval rules (resolving RAG.md §6's five OPEN rules)

These five rules had "no published precedent" and were left open by the lit
review [LIT-BACKED: RAG.md §7.1]. They are resolved here as **binding defaults**
because the frontier's own matched-corpus arm (§2.3a) cannot be built without
them; **P3-D-RAGC inherits them verbatim and instantiates the factorial cells on
top; it may refine a rule only by registered amendment, never silently**
[STIPULATED: P3F-A4].

**Rule 1 — source-content derivation + information-parity criterion.** One pinned
source snapshot (content hash) per vertical/corpus. Each arm's indexed objects are
derived by a **pinned, automated, seed-pinned derivation script** (kernel records
via the standing mint pipeline version; passages via a pinned chunker
(size/overlap pinned); triples via a pinned extractor; tool schemas via a pinned
generator) — human curation inside a derivation is permitted only if its hours are
logged to KOT-LIFE/1 and disclosed. Parity is enforced as four MEASURED checks,
and no further: (a) **source parity** — every arm's derivation consumes the
identical snapshot, no arm-specific extra sources; (b) **byte cap parity** — every
arm's packed indexed-object bytes ≤ the same store-byte allocation within B_k
(equal cap, not forced-equal usage); (c) **screen parity** — the §2.2
decontamination screen applies to every arm's derived objects identically;
(d) **effort disclosure** — authoring/curation/build compute per arm co-reported
(no fudged exchange rate, consistent with KOT-LIFE/1's no-common-unit rule).
Residual honesty line, carried verbatim on every consuming claim: *information
parity beyond these four checks cannot be certified — converting one snapshot
into different object types changes the retrieval problem itself; this is a
standing limitation, not a solved problem* [STIPULATED: P3F-A4; the RAG.md §6.1
scope finding adopted].

**Rule 2 — identical vs architecture-specific retrievers.** Two mandatory cells,
each with a distinct claim licence: the **NATIVE cell** — each arm retrieves the
way its architecture natively does (kernel addressing for S; BM25/dense for RAG)
under the same resource budgets — is what enters **F(B_k)** (the frontier is
"the best each architecture can do at the budget"); the **SHARED cell** — one
pinned retriever+index over a common text serialisation of every arm's objects —
is an **attribution instrument only** (isolates content representation at fixed
retrieval; never a frontier member, because it structurally handicaps arms whose
native retrieval is not dense-over-text). Every consuming prereg names which cell
licenses which sentence [STIPULATED: P3F-A4].

**Rule 3 — retrieved-token/context budgets + ordering parity.** Pinned per rung:
max retrieved tokens per query (identical across arms), max context length,
context assembly order = retrieval-score-descending (no arm may hand-place gold
material at context edges — the position confound), and a position-shuffle
control cell lives in RAGC's factorial [LIT-BACKED: RAG.md §6.4,
Lost-in-the-Middle; STIPULATED: P3F-A4].

**Rule 4 — generator / executor / retry / tuning parity.** Where the comparison
is store-vs-store (matched-corpus arm vs S), the generator checkpoint, decoding
parameters, retry budget (same max-k), abstention accounting, and tool-executor
semantics are IDENTICAL across arms; tuning ≤ T_k per arm (§3.3). Where the
comparison is whole-system frontier (free-corpus arm), the generator may differ —
that arm's licence is "a better baseline exists at this budget", not attribution
[STIPULATED: P3F-A4].

**Rule 5 — construction leakage + authoring effort.** Every construction pass
(embedding sweeps, LLM extraction, selector training — the TinyAgent DeBERTa
lesson [LIT-BACKED: RAG.md §1]) is booked as build compute in KOT-LIFE/1 and its
artifacts' bytes in KOT-SIZE/2 figures (1)/(5) on EVERY arm identically.
Order-of-operations rule: derivation/construction pipelines are frozen
(content-hashed) BEFORE any frozen-suite item is drawn; the §2.2 decontamination
screen applies to construction INPUTS as well as outputs; a store or index whose
construction post-dates suite exposure is VOID for W1 use [STIPULATED: P3F-A4].

---

## 6. NL-boundary discipline (no smuggled oracle)

- DECISION: every frontier comparator consumes **natural benchmark text** — the
  NL-input integrity rule (ASM-0808) applies to comparators exactly as to S.
  Pure-neural and RAG arms satisfy this natively: their NL handling is part of
  their measured product, exactly as S's parser is part of S's
  [STIPULATED: ASM-0808 applied to comparators; P3F-A2].
- DECISION: the frontier NEVER receives gold parses, gold subgraphs, gold record
  addresses, or gold passages. One labelled exception class exists for
  DECOMPOSITION only: an `oracle-diagnostic` comparator (e.g. gold-passage
  injection as a retrieval ceiling) may be built for a G2/G3 analysis, is
  labelled per ASM-0814, **never enters F(B_k)**, and licenses no W1 claim
  [STIPULATED: ASM-0814 applied; P3F-A2].
- Asymmetry, stated so nobody "fixes" it later: the NLB gate (P3-E-NLB-1) binds
  KERNEL-store addressing from natural input — a measured wall of the kernel
  front-end [MEASURED: l3a-parse/a5-nl FAILs] — and does NOT bind the frontier's
  baselines. Weakening the frontier to share the kernel's wall would fake
  exactly the win W1 exists to make honest [STIPULATED: P3F-A2].

---

## 7. Threat-model counters implemented in the builder (feeds P3-D-THREAT)

### 7.1 Inherited channels, with their mechanised counters here

| Gaming channel (programme §2.0) | Counter in this design |
|---|---|
| Budget shopping | §1.2: anchor-derived B_k pinned at rung entry, immutable after first S measurement |
| Comparator dodging | §2 pinned enumeration rule + §7.2 nomination window; frontier version bump forced by any post-freeze release |
| Warm-cache-only reporting | §1.1/§3 S3: warm AND cold both measured and both admissibility-checked (P3-D-HW) |
| Batch-size shopping | pinned concurrency distribution (KOT-COST/2(g)) in every S3 measurement |
| Output-length inflation/deflation | pinned max-token + stop discipline per benchmark (KOT-COST/2(j)) |
| Tuning asymmetry | §3.3 T_k meter, per-arm, audit-trailed in KOT-LIFE/1 |
| Packaging games | all bytes via the ONE canonical packing script (KOT-SIZE/2), frozen base image |

### 7.2 The nomination window (comparator-dodging counter, concrete)

- DECISION: from frontier-build start until frontier freeze, ANY party (maintainer,
  reviewer, external referee) may nominate an open reproducible system or config
  for F(B_k); the builder must either admit it (measure under the common ledger)
  or publish a one-line refusal reason (licence, irreproducibility, static byte
  infeasibility). Post-freeze nominations queue for the next frontier version
  [STIPULATED: ASM-0812 comparator-pinning clause extended; P3F-A2].

### 7.3 The NEW channel this design must name: weak-builder gaming

The party building S also builds S's opponents — under-optimising the frontier
manufactures a fake W1 win. This is the frontier-side mirror of the answer-key
trap, and it gets first-class treatment:

- DECISION: four counters [STIPULATED: ASM-0812 threat-model extension; P3F-A5]:
  1. **External-reproduction acceptance tests (F-CAL A2, §8):** pinned transforms
     must reproduce published reference behaviour (e.g. quantisation retention
     for a same-class model/method) within a pre-registered tolerance — a builder
     that cannot match known results cannot certify a frontier.
  2. **Full search disclosure:** every builder run publishes the complete config
     log — candidates enumerated, dropped (and why), compute spent per arm — the
     no-silent-caps rule applied to the search itself.
  3. **External referee pass:** the frozen frontier spec + build log goes through
     the standing GPT-5.6-class external review before any W1 experiment freezes
     against it.
  4. **Open nomination (§7.2)** — outsiders can strengthen the frontier we would
     be tempted to weaken.
- Honesty line carried on every W1 claim regardless: **baseline-search
  completeness remains a standing limitation** (programme §7.3) — W1's licensed
  claim is already narrowed to "every pre-registered, reproducible open comparator
  SEARCHED under budget B_k", never a universal [STIPULATED: ASM-0810 restated,

not new].

---

## 8. Validation and falsifiability of the builder itself (F-CAL)

The builder is an INSTRUMENT and gets instrument gates before it judges anything
— the same discipline P3-E-CAL applies to the index. New experiment bead
**P3-E-FCAL** (co-scheduled with P3-E-CAL, same free-pool compute class; §13),
pre-registered with these acceptance criteria [STIPULATED: P3F-A5]:

- **A1 — monotonicity (the builder must add value):** at the R-1 anchor budget,
  the builder's best optimised entry must satisfy dev-index(best) ≥
  dev-index(naive anchor baseline) with LCB95 of the difference > −ε_A1 (a
  pre-registered non-inferiority margin; single one-sided CI per §2.5(4)). A
  builder whose "optimised" frontier loses to the untouched anchor is broken.
- **A2 — external reproduction:** for two pinned (model, quantisation-method)
  pairs with published retention figures, the builder's measured retention falls
  within a pre-registered tolerance band of the published figure. Catches silent
  tooling breakage AND weak-builder gaming (§7.3).
- **A3 — repeatability:** two independent builder runs (different seeds, same
  grid) produce envelopes agreeing within a pre-registered band (dev-index ± ε_A3
  at each budget point) and select the same frozen configs or documented ties.
  An unstable selection rule cannot be frozen.
- **Kill / fail-closed rule:** if A1 or A2 fails after one pre-registered repair
  cycle, **no W1 experiment may cite F(B_k)** — every G4 [EXP] stays blocked
  (the builder fails closed, ERR-style, like everything else in this programme).
  A3 failure forces selection-rule redesign before freeze.

Falsifiability of the design as a whole: FRONT/1 is falsified as an instrument by
its own F-CAL gates; the frontier it produces is falsified per rung by W1's own
test; and the design's central stipulation — that a best-effort-optimised,
honestly-ledgered baseline frontier at 100M–2B is CONSTRUCTIBLE inside the free
compute pool — is falsified if the §10 budget is exhausted before A1–A3 pass
[STIPULATED: P3F-A5].

- PREMISE: the free pool spans ARC, AIRR Gateway (application in flight per
  programme §6), TRC, Modal-for-Academics, ~$1k API credits [LIT-BACKED:
  docs/next/free-compute-recon.md, source-verified 2026-07-10, via programme §6].
- Cost structure by stage [STIPULATED: P3F-A5-annex, estimates not commitments]:
  quantisation + packing are CPU/single-GPU trivial (hours); RAG index builds are
  CPU + one embed pass; the dominant spend is (a) TTC evaluation (bounded by the
  N ≤ 64 cap and the dev mini-suite screen) and (b) recovery training for pruned

  builder total ≤ ~40 GPU-h + T_1 = 25 GPU-h/arm caps (fits the ≤50 GPU-h/family
  discipline of programme §4); **R-2** ≤ ~150 GPU-h total, AIRR/ARC. Local-box

   — four measured checks + disclosure, residual asymmetry named (§5 rule 1).

## 0. Contract: what the frontier-builder is, and is not

The frontier-builder is the **standing instrument that constructs F(B_k)** — the
pre-registered, reproducible comparator set, and the measured **staircase/Pareto
envelope**, that any Programme-3 candidate system S must beat under W1 at budget
vector B_k [STIPULATED: P3F-A1, operationalising ASM-0812 clause "the comparator
set F(B_k) is built by a standing neural frontier-builder"]. It is one of the
frozen artifacts of KOT-FAIR/2 (programme §2.4 "the frontier-builder spec +
comparator pinning rule") and it **feeds every G4 [EXP]** (programme §5 Phase-2
blocking rule).

What it is NOT, with the owning bead named:

| Not this | Owner |
|---|---|
| The index (suite, normalisation, analysis plan) | P3-D-INDEX |
| The measurement rig (hardware repeatability, warm/cold, energy counters) | P3-D-HW |
| The per-rung donor/twin **pins** (named model+revision list, twin recipe) | P3-D-BASE — FRONT defines the enumeration RULE (§2.1); BASE pins the roster |
| The factorial attribution control cells (derangement, label-permutation, …) | P3-D-RAGC — FRONT resolves the five shared matched-retrieval rules (§5) as binding defaults RAGC inherits |
| The candidate system S or any architecture family | P3-D-VL/GNN/RULE/PS/DD/GU |

Relation to the measured walls, stated up front:

- PREMISE: the programme has ZERO audited end-task wins over the kernel-as-text
  null attributable to kernel content; both value theses stand INCONCLUSIVE-PENDING
  [MEASURED: docs/next/feasibility-synthesis.md §0]. The frontier-builder exists to
  make any FUTURE win claim survivable: W1 is only as strong as the frontier it
  beats.
- PREMISE: both measured NL crossings of the kernel front-end FAILED (l3a-parse
  47.6% retention; a5-nl 41.6% + S2 dangerous-wrong fired) [MEASURED:
  registry/verdicts/l3a-parse.json + a5-nl.json]. Consequence for THIS design: the
  frontier's comparators consume natural benchmark text natively and are NEVER
  gated by NLB — the NL wall is the KERNEL side's cost, and the frontier must not
  be weakened to share it (§6).
- PREMISE: this 28-source sweep found no published 100M–2B retrieval/tool-use win
  that survives a deployment-bytes-inclusive, matched-total-resource ledger
  ("none found here", not "none exists") [LIT-BACKED: RAG.md §1 synthesis,
  28 sources fetched at primary venue 2026-07-11]. The frontier we must build is
  therefore genuinely NOVEL accounting: it is constructed, not cited
  [STIPULATED: ASM-0812, executed here; P3F-A1].

## 1. Objects: budget vectors, F(B_k), and the envelope

### 1.1 The budget-vector grammar

Per W1 (programme §1.4), a budget vector is
`B_k = (bytes_max, p95_latency_max, energy_per_query_max, mem_max)` where:

- `bytes_max` = KOT-SIZE/2 figure-(1) canonically-packed minimal artifact bytes
  (model + tokenizer + any store/index/verifier/selector/retriever weights + engine
  + anything beyond the frozen base image; remote dependencies count or the claim
  is void, per ASM-0810);
- `p95_latency_max` = end-to-end p95 per query under the pinned P3-D-HW
  concurrency distribution, warm AND cold both admissible-checked;
- `energy_per_query_max` = whole-run integrated energy / queries (RAPL + NVML per
  KOT-COST/2(d));
- `mem_max` = (peak accelerator memory, host RSS) pair.

All four components are measured by the P3-D-HW rig; the builder consumes that rig,
it does not define it [STIPULATED: P3F-A1].

### 1.2 The budget derivation rule (the anti-budget-shopping counter)

- DECISION: **one primary budget vector per rung**, derived mechanically from the
  rung's named anchor model and pinned at RUNG ENTRY, before any candidate system
  S is measured at that rung: `B_k := the anchor's own measured resource vector`
  — bytes = the anchor's canonically-packed bf16 bytes; latency/energy/memory =
  the anchor's measured figures on the pinned rig under the pinned index-suite
  workload, at multiplier ×1.0 on every component [STIPULATED: ASM-0810
  operationalised; P3F-A1]. Rung
  anchors are the programme's existing orientation anchors (R-1 = SmolLM2-135M,
  R-2 = SmolLM2-360M, R-3 = SmolLM2-1.7B; programme §4), pinned as name+revision
  by P3-D-BASE.
- DECISION: additional budget vectors at a rung (e.g. a tighter-bytes point for
  W2 shrink work) may be registered ONLY before any S candidate is measured at
  that rung; after first S measurement, the budget set at that rung is immutable
  for that index version. If S does not fit B_k, S fails ADMISSIBILITY — there is
  no post-hoc budget inflation, and no "S defines its own budget" path
  [STIPULATED: ASM-0810/ASM-0812 operationalised; P3F-A1; counters the
  threat-model "budget shopping" channel, programme §2.0].
- Rationale (why anchor-derived, not S-derived): deriving B_k from S's measured
  resources would let S's packaging choices move the goalposts — the review's
  band-gaming concern in new clothes. Anchor-derived budgets are computable before
  any architecture exists and are exactly what "comparably-resourced" means to an
  outside reader [STIPULATED: P3F-A1].

### 1.3 F(B_k) and the envelope — formal semantics

- **F(B_k)** = the set of comparator systems C such that (i) C is built by this
  builder from the pinned search space (§2–§4) or nominated per §7.2, (ii) C is
  ADMISSIBLE (fits every component of B_k, warm and cold, measured under
  KOT-SIZE/2 + KOT-COST/2), and (iii) C is pinned (config hash, weights hash,
  seeds, decoding params, index hash, verifier hash) at frontier freeze
  [STIPULATED: P3F-A2].
- **The envelope** = the staircase of (index vs bytes) and (index vs cost) over
  ALL measured points, admissible and not, dominated and not — published in full
  with the Pareto subset marked and B_k marked. W1 = S significantly above the
  envelope at its budget (programme §1.4 reporting rule). Dominated and
  inadmissible points are REPORTED, never silently dropped (no-silent-caps rule)
  [STIPULATED: P3F-A2].
- **Statistics interface** (consumes P3-D-INDEX's §2.5 analysis plan, does not
  define it): W1's test is simultaneous `LCB95(INDEX(S) − INDEX(C)) > δ_k` over
  every C ∈ F(B_k), FWER-controlled. Note for the analysis plan: since the index
  is scalar, beating the arg-max comparator implies beating the rest; a max-t-type
  simultaneous procedure over F(B_k) loses little power however large F is — so a
  RICH frontier is statistically conservative for S, and the builder has no
  incentive to keep F small [STIPULATED: P3F-A2].
- **Version discipline:** F(B_k) is content-hashed at freeze. Any change —
  adding a comparator, re-tuning one, a new external model release — is a frontier
  VERSION bump that re-baselines W1 at that rung; nothing folds in silently
  (mirrors the encoder ALGORITHM_VERSION discipline and LiveBench release
  discipline) [STIPULATED: P3F-A2].

---

## 2. The comparator search space (ASM-0812's five clauses made concrete)

The search space is a **pinned grid**, not an open-ended search — completeness is
bounded and DISCLOSED (§7.3), never claimed.

### 2.1 C1 — pure-neural donors (enumeration rule; P3-D-BASE pins names)

- DECISION: eligibility rule — open weights, redistributable licence sufficient
  for evaluation, pinned name + revision (HF commit hash), tokenizer + any chat
  template included in bytes, base AND instruct variants where both exist.
  Eligibility is decided by **measured packed bytes under the transform grid**,
  never by parameter count (a 2.6B model whose Q3 quantisation fits bytes_max is
  eligible; a 1B model that doesn't fit isn't) [STIPULATED: ASM-0812(i)
  operationalised; P3F-A2].
- Candidate roster to hand P3-D-BASE (candidates, not pins): SmolLM2-135M/360M/
  1.7B (±instruct), Qwen2.5-0.5B/1.5B (±instruct), Llama-3.2-1B (±instruct),
  Pythia-160M/410M/1B/1.4B, OLMo-2-1B-class, plus any stronger open release that
  appears before freeze via the nomination window (§7.2). Anchor caveats carried
  from P3-LR-TINY: released anchors are heavily overtrained (SmolLM2-1.7B ~6,470
  tok/param) and are PINS, never twins [LIT-BACKED: TINY.md §2/§9].

### 2.2 C2 — budget-optimising transforms

- **Quantisation ladder (cheap, CPU/1-GPU):** pinned method×level grid —
  llama.cpp k-quants {Q8_0, Q5_K_M, Q4_K_M, Q3_K_M} and one calibration-based
  int4 (GPTQ or AWQ, one pinned tool+version) — applied to every C1 donor whose
  quantised bytes fit bytes_max. Tool versions and conversion scripts pinned;
  quantisation is symmetric across arms per §1.2's standing rule (programme §1.2:
  "if the neurosymbolic arm quantises, every comparator gets the same best-effort
  quantisation") [STIPULATED: P3F-A2].
- **Structured pruning + recovery:** depth/width structured pruning at pinned
  fractions {12.5%, 25%, 50%} WITH retrained recovery/distillation inside the
  tuning budget T_k (§3.3) — the strongest-published-practice requirement the
  review imposed on H-DD's comparator (iv) applies to the frontier's own pruned
  entries identically (pruning-without-recovery is too weak) [STIPULATED:
  P3F-A2]. Method selection defers to P3-LR-SURG's best-practice findings when
  that review lands (in-flight); until then pruned entries are OPTIONAL grid
  members, never load-bearing for a frontier freeze.
- **Distillation:** teacher→student KD from an in-roster larger donor into a
  budget-fitting student, capped by T_k. Expensive; eligible at R-2+ only unless
  free-pool compute allows at R-1 [STIPULATED: P3F-A2].

### 2.3 C3 — neural + retrieval (RAG / tool-use)

Two distinct arm types, both built on a **pinned standard harness** (FlashRAG-class;
release pinned at freeze — the v1/v2 count discrepancy lesson) [LIT-BACKED:
RAG.md §3]:

- **(a) The matched-corpus RAG arm (mandatory whenever S uses a store):** the
  §2.3(iii) arm — same pinned source snapshot as S's store, same byte budgets,
  retriever cells BM25 + one pinned small dense embedder (embedder weights + index
  bytes charged to bytes_max), built under the five matched-retrieval rules of §5.
  This arm is simultaneously a frontier member and the strongest attribution
  control (ASM-0812) [STIPULATED: P3F-A4].
- **(b) The free-corpus RAG arm (pure frontier point):** the builder may choose
  ANY corpus/datastore for the baseline, scaled to fill the remaining byte budget
  (the datastore-scaling lever: datastore size improves performance monotonically
  [LIT-BACKED: RAG.md §1, MassiveDS]) — because W1's frontier is "the best
  baseline that fits the budget", not "the baseline that mirrors S". Index bytes,
  build compute, and embedder bytes all charged [STIPULATED: P3F-A2].
- **Adaptive retrieval** (retrieve-only-when-needed, popularity/confidence-
  thresholded) is in the grid — it improved accuracy while CUTTING cost in the
  one directly-relevant published result [LIT-BACKED: RAG.md §1, Mallen].
- **Tool-use cells:** a function-calling fine-tune (APIGen-style verified
  synthetic data direction) enters only where the index suite has tool-relevant
  components; any separately trained selector (the TinyAgent DeBERTa lesson) is
  charged bytes + build compute [LIT-BACKED: RAG.md §1; STIPULATED: P3F-A2].

### 2.4 C4 — adaptive test-time compute (the executable recipe: §4)

### 2.5 C5 — from-scratch twins

Required only where S itself trains from scratch (H-GU); recipe and pins are
P3-D-BASE/P3-LR-TINY property (isoFLOP placement, same-data-same-order, ≥3 paired
seeds); the builder only ADMITS the twin into F(B_k) and measures it under the
common ledger [STIPULATED: P3F-A2].

### 2.6 Composition rule

Transforms compose along a pinned lattice: {C1 donor} × {quant level} × {± C3
retrieval} × {± C4 TTC policy}; pruning/distillation entries compose with quant
only. The FULL composed grid at R-1 is enumerable (≈ low hundreds of static
candidates before byte-filtering); §3's staged screen keeps measurement inside the
free-compute pool [STIPULATED: P3F-A2].

## 3. The builder procedure (six stages, executable)

460	|---|---|
   461	| Budget shopping | §1.2: anchor-derived B_k pinned at rung entry, immutable after first S measurement |
   462	| Comparator dodging | §2 pinned enumeration rule + §7.2 nomination window; frontier version bump forced by any post-freeze release |
   463	| Warm-cache-only reporting | §1.1/§3 S3: warm AND cold both measured and both admissibility-checked (P3-D-HW) |
   464	| Batch-size shopping | pinned concurrency distribution (KOT-COST/2(g)) in every S3 measurement |
   465	| Output-length inflation/deflation | pinned max-token + stop discipline per benchmark (KOT-COST/2(j)) |
   466	| Tuning asymmetry | §3.3 T_k meter, per-arm, audit-trailed in KOT-LIFE/1 |
   467	| Packaging games | all bytes via the ONE canonical packing script (KOT-SIZE/2), frozen base image |

   469	### 7.2 The nomination window (comparator-dodging counter, concrete)
   470	
   471	- DECISION: from frontier-build start until frontier freeze, ANY party (maintainer,
   472	  reviewer, external referee) may nominate an open reproducible system or config
   473	  for F(B_k); the builder must either admit it (measure under the common ledger)
   474	  or publish a one-line refusal reason (licence, irreproducibility, static byte
   475	  infeasibility). Post-freeze nominations queue for the next frontier version
   476	  [STIPULATED: ASM-0812 comparator-pinning clause extended; P3F-A2].

   478	### 7.3 The NEW channel this design must name: weak-builder gaming
   479	
   480	The party building S also builds S's opponents — under-optimising the frontier
   481	manufactures a fake W1 win. This is the frontier-side mirror of the answer-key
   482	trap, and it gets first-class treatment:
   483	
   484	- DECISION: four counters [STIPULATED: ASM-0812 threat-model extension; P3F-A5]:
   485	  1. **External-reproduction acceptance tests (F-CAL A2, §8):** pinned transforms
   486	     must reproduce published reference behaviour (e.g. quantisation retention
   487	     for a same-class model/method) within a pre-registered tolerance — a builder
   488	     that cannot match known results cannot certify a frontier.
   489	  2. **Full search disclosure:** every builder run publishes the complete config
   490	     log — candidates enumerated, dropped (and why), compute spent per arm — the
   491	     no-silent-caps rule applied to the search itself.
   492	  3. **External referee pass:** the frozen frontier spec + build log goes through
   493	     the standing GPT-5.6-class external review before any W1 experiment freezes
   494	     against it.
   495	  4. **Open nomination (§7.2)** — outsiders can strengthen the frontier we would
   496	     be tempted to weaken.
   497	- Honesty line carried on every W1 claim regardless: **baseline-search
   498	  completeness remains a standing limitation** (programme §7.3) — W1's licensed
   499	  claim is already narrowed to "every pre-registered, reproducible open comparator
   500	  SEARCHED under budget B_k", never a universal [STIPULATED: ASM-0810 restated,

   505	## 8. Validation and falsifiability of the builder itself (F-CAL)
   506	
   507	The builder is an INSTRUMENT and gets instrument gates before it judges anything
   508	— the same discipline P3-E-CAL applies to the index. New experiment bead
   509	**P3-E-FCAL** (co-scheduled with P3-E-CAL, same free-pool compute class; §13),
   510	pre-registered with these acceptance criteria [STIPULATED: P3F-A5]:
   511	
   512	- **A1 — monotonicity (the builder must add value):** at the R-1 anchor budget,
   513	  the builder's best optimised entry must satisfy dev-index(best) ≥
   514	  dev-index(naive anchor baseline) with LCB95 of the difference > −ε_A1 (a
   515	  pre-registered non-inferiority margin; single one-sided CI per §2.5(4)). A
   516	  builder whose "optimised" frontier loses to the untouched anchor is broken.
   517	- **A2 — external reproduction:** for two pinned (model, quantisation-method)
   518	  pairs with published retention figures, the builder's measured retention falls
   519	  within a pre-registered tolerance band of the published figure. Catches silent
   520	  tooling breakage AND weak-builder gaming (§7.3).
   521	- **A3 — repeatability:** two independent builder runs (different seeds, same
   522	  grid) produce envelopes agreeing within a pre-registered band (dev-index ± ε_A3
   523	  at each budget point) and select the same frozen configs or documented ties.
   524	  An unstable selection rule cannot be frozen.
   525	- **Kill / fail-closed rule:** if A1 or A2 fails after one pre-registered repair
   526	  cycle, **no W1 experiment may cite F(B_k)** — every G4 [EXP] stays blocked
   527	  (the builder fails closed, ERR-style, like everything else in this programme).
   528	  A3 failure forces selection-rule redesign before freeze.
   529	
   530	Falsifiability of the design as a whole: FRONT/1 is falsified as an instrument by
   531	its own F-CAL gates; the frontier it produces is falsified per rung by W1's own
   532	test; and the design's central stipulation — that a best-effort-optimised,
   533	honestly-ledgered baseline frontier at 100M–2B is CONSTRUCTIBLE inside the free
   534	compute pool — is falsified if the §10 budget is exhausted before A1–A3 pass
   535	[STIPULATED: P3F-A5].

    35	## 0. Contract: what the frontier-builder is, and is not
    36	
    37	The frontier-builder is the **standing instrument that constructs F(B_k)** — the
    38	pre-registered, reproducible comparator set, and the measured **staircase/Pareto
    39	envelope**, that any Programme-3 candidate system S must beat under W1 at budget
    40	vector B_k [STIPULATED: P3F-A1, operationalising ASM-0812 clause "the comparator
    41	set F(B_k) is built by a standing neural frontier-builder"]. It is one of the
    42	frozen artifacts of KOT-FAIR/2 (programme §2.4 "the frontier-builder spec +
    43	comparator pinning rule") and it **feeds every G4 [EXP]** (programme §5 Phase-2
    44	blocking rule).
    45	
    46	What it is NOT, with the owning bead named:
    47	
    48	| Not this | Owner |
    49	|---|---|
    50	| The index (suite, normalisation, analysis plan) | P3-D-INDEX |
    51	| The measurement rig (hardware repeatability, warm/cold, energy counters) | P3-D-HW |
    52	| The per-rung donor/twin **pins** (named model+revision list, twin recipe) | P3-D-BASE — FRONT defines the enumeration RULE (§2.1); BASE pins the roster |
    53	| The factorial attribution control cells (derangement, label-permutation, …) | P3-D-RAGC — FRONT resolves the five shared matched-retrieval rules (§5) as binding defaults RAGC inherits |
    54	| The candidate system S or any architecture family | P3-D-VL/GNN/RULE/PS/DD/GU |

    77	
    78	## 1. Objects: budget vectors, F(B_k), and the envelope
    79	
    80	### 1.1 The budget-vector grammar
    81	
    82	Per W1 (programme §1.4), a budget vector is
    83	`B_k = (bytes_max, p95_latency_max, energy_per_query_max, mem_max)` where:
    84	
    85	- `bytes_max` = KOT-SIZE/2 figure-(1) canonically-packed minimal artifact bytes
    86	  (model + tokenizer + any store/index/verifier/selector/retriever weights + engine
    87	  + anything beyond the frozen base image; remote dependencies count or the claim
    88	  is void, per ASM-0810);
    89	- `p95_latency_max` = end-to-end p95 per query under the pinned P3-D-HW
    90	  concurrency distribution, warm AND cold both admissible-checked;
    91	- `energy_per_query_max` = whole-run integrated energy / queries (RAPL + NVML per
    92	  KOT-COST/2(d));
    93	- `mem_max` = (peak accelerator memory, host RSS) pair.
    94	
    95	All four components are measured by the P3-D-HW rig; the builder consumes that rig,
    96	it does not define it [STIPULATED: P3F-A1].
    97	
    98	### 1.2 The budget derivation rule (the anti-budget-shopping counter)
    99	
   100	- DECISION: **one primary budget vector per rung**, derived mechanically from the
   101	  rung's named anchor model and pinned at RUNG ENTRY, before any candidate system
   102	  S is measured at that rung: `B_k := the anchor's own measured resource vector`
   103	  — bytes = the anchor's canonically-packed bf16 bytes; latency/energy/memory =
   104	  the anchor's measured figures on the pinned rig under the pinned index-suite
   105	  workload, at multiplier ×1.0 on every component [STIPULATED: ASM-0810
   106	  operationalised; P3F-A1]. Rung
   107	  anchors are the programme's existing orientation anchors (R-1 = SmolLM2-135M,
   108	  R-2 = SmolLM2-360M, R-3 = SmolLM2-1.7B; programme §4), pinned as name+revision
   109	  by P3-D-BASE.
   110	- DECISION: additional budget vectors at a rung (e.g. a tighter-bytes point for
   111	  W2 shrink work) may be registered ONLY before any S candidate is measured at
   112	  that rung; after first S measurement, the budget set at that rung is immutable
   113	  for that index version. If S does not fit B_k, S fails ADMISSIBILITY — there is
   114	  no post-hoc budget inflation, and no "S defines its own budget" path
   115	  [STIPULATED: ASM-0810/ASM-0812 operationalised; P3F-A1; counters the
   116	  threat-model "budget shopping" channel, programme §2.0].
   117	- Rationale (why anchor-derived, not S-derived): deriving B_k from S's measured
   118	  resources would let S's packaging choices move the goalposts — the review's
   119	  band-gaming concern in new clothes. Anchor-derived budgets are computable before
   120	  any architecture exists and are exactly what "comparably-resourced" means to an
   121	  outside reader [STIPULATED: P3F-A1].
   122	
   123	### 1.3 F(B_k) and the envelope — formal semantics
   124	
   125	- **F(B_k)** = the set of comparator systems C such that (i) C is built by this
   126	  builder from the pinned search space (§2–§4) or nominated per §7.2, (ii) C is
   127	  ADMISSIBLE (fits every component of B_k, warm and cold, measured under
   128	  KOT-SIZE/2 + KOT-COST/2), and (iii) C is pinned (config hash, weights hash,
   129	  seeds, decoding params, index hash, verifier hash) at frontier freeze
   130	  [STIPULATED: P3F-A2].
   131	- **The envelope** = the staircase of (index vs bytes) and (index vs cost) over
   132	  ALL measured points, admissible and not, dominated and not — published in full
   133	  with the Pareto subset marked and B_k marked. W1 = S significantly above the
   134	  envelope at its budget (programme §1.4 reporting rule). Dominated and
   135	  inadmissible points are REPORTED, never silently dropped (no-silent-caps rule)
   136	  [STIPULATED: P3F-A2].
   137	- **Statistics interface** (consumes P3-D-INDEX's §2.5 analysis plan, does not
   138	  define it): W1's test is simultaneous `LCB95(INDEX(S) − INDEX(C)) > δ_k` over
   139	  every C ∈ F(B_k), FWER-controlled. Note for the analysis plan: since the index
   140	  is scalar, beating the arg-max comparator implies beating the rest; a max-t-type
   141	  simultaneous procedure over F(B_k) loses little power however large F is — so a
   142	  RICH frontier is statistically conservative for S, and the builder has no
   143	  incentive to keep F small [STIPULATED: P3F-A2].
   144	- **Version discipline:** F(B_k) is content-hashed at freeze. Any change —
   145	  adding a comparator, re-tuning one, a new external model release — is a frontier
   146	  VERSION bump that re-baselines W1 at that rung; nothing folds in silently
   147	  (mirrors the encoder ALGORITHM_VERSION discipline and LiveBench release
   148	  discipline) [STIPULATED: P3F-A2].
   149	
   150	---
   151	
   152	## 2. The comparator search space (ASM-0812's five clauses made concrete)
   153	
   154	The search space is a **pinned grid**, not an open-ended search — completeness is
   155	bounded and DISCLOSED (§7.3), never claimed.
   156	
   157	### 2.1 C1 — pure-neural donors (enumeration rule; P3-D-BASE pins names)
   158	
   159	- DECISION: eligibility rule — open weights, redistributable licence sufficient
   160	  for evaluation, pinned name + revision (HF commit hash), tokenizer + any chat
   161	  template included in bytes, base AND instruct variants where both exist.
   162	  Eligibility is decided by **measured packed bytes under the transform grid**,
   163	  never by parameter count (a 2.6B model whose Q3 quantisation fits bytes_max is
   164	  eligible; a 1B model that doesn't fit isn't) [STIPULATED: ASM-0812(i)
   165	  operationalised; P3F-A2].
   166	- Candidate roster to hand P3-D-BASE (candidates, not pins): SmolLM2-135M/360M/
   167	  1.7B (±instruct), Qwen2.5-0.5B/1.5B (±instruct), Llama-3.2-1B (±instruct),
   168	  Pythia-160M/410M/1B/1.4B, OLMo-2-1B-class, plus any stronger open release that
   169	  appears before freeze via the nomination window (§7.2). Anchor caveats carried
   170	  from P3-LR-TINY: released anchors are heavily overtrained (SmolLM2-1.7B ~6,470
   171	  tok/param) and are PINS, never twins [LIT-BACKED: TINY.md §2/§9].
   172	
   173	### 2.2 C2 — budget-optimising transforms
   174	
   175	- **Quantisation ladder (cheap, CPU/1-GPU):** pinned method×level grid —
   176	  llama.cpp k-quants {Q8_0, Q5_K_M, Q4_K_M, Q3_K_M} and one calibration-based
   177	  int4 (GPTQ or AWQ, one pinned tool+version) — applied to every C1 donor whose
   178	  quantised bytes fit bytes_max. Tool versions and conversion scripts pinned;
   179	  quantisation is symmetric across arms per §1.2's standing rule (programme §1.2:
   180	  "if the neurosymbolic arm quantises, every comparator gets the same best-effort
   181	  quantisation") [STIPULATED: P3F-A2].
   182	- **Structured pruning + recovery:** depth/width structured pruning at pinned
   183	  fractions {12.5%, 25%, 50%} WITH retrained recovery/distillation inside the
   184	  tuning budget T_k (§3.3) — the strongest-published-practice requirement the
   185	  review imposed on H-DD's comparator (iv) applies to the frontier's own pruned
   186	  entries identically (pruning-without-recovery is too weak) [STIPULATED:
   187	  P3F-A2]. Method selection defers to P3-LR-SURG's best-practice findings when
   188	  that review lands (in-flight); until then pruned entries are OPTIONAL grid
   189	  members, never load-bearing for a frontier freeze.
   190	- **Distillation:** teacher→student KD from an in-roster larger donor into a
   191	  budget-fitting student, capped by T_k. Expensive; eligible at R-2+ only unless
   192	  free-pool compute allows at R-1 [STIPULATED: P3F-A2].
   193	
   194	### 2.3 C3 — neural + retrieval (RAG / tool-use)
   195	
   196	Two distinct arm types, both built on a **pinned standard harness** (FlashRAG-class;
   197	release pinned at freeze — the v1/v2 count discrepancy lesson) [LIT-BACKED:
   198	RAG.md §3]:
   199	
   200	- **(a) The matched-corpus RAG arm (mandatory whenever S uses a store):** the
   201	  §2.3(iii) arm — same pinned source snapshot as S's store, same byte budgets,
   202	  retriever cells BM25 + one pinned small dense embedder (embedder weights + index
   203	  bytes charged to bytes_max), built under the five matched-retrieval rules of §5.
   204	  This arm is simultaneously a frontier member and the strongest attribution
   205	  control (ASM-0812) [STIPULATED: P3F-A4].
   206	- **(b) The free-corpus RAG arm (pure frontier point):** the builder may choose
   207	  ANY corpus/datastore for the baseline, scaled to fill the remaining byte budget
   208	  (the datastore-scaling lever: datastore size improves performance monotonically
   209	  [LIT-BACKED: RAG.md §1, MassiveDS]) — because W1's frontier is "the best
   210	  baseline that fits the budget", not "the baseline that mirrors S". Index bytes,
   211	  build compute, and embedder bytes all charged [STIPULATED: P3F-A2].
   212	- **Adaptive retrieval** (retrieve-only-when-needed, popularity/confidence-
   213	  thresholded) is in the grid — it improved accuracy while CUTTING cost in the
   214	  one directly-relevant published result [LIT-BACKED: RAG.md §1, Mallen].
   215	- **Tool-use cells:** a function-calling fine-tune (APIGen-style verified
   216	  synthetic data direction) enters only where the index suite has tool-relevant
   217	  components; any separately trained selector (the TinyAgent DeBERTa lesson) is
   218	  charged bytes + build compute [LIT-BACKED: RAG.md §1; STIPULATED: P3F-A2].
   219	
   220	### 2.4 C4 — adaptive test-time compute (the executable recipe: §4)

220	### 2.4 C4 — adaptive test-time compute (the executable recipe: §4)
   221	
   222	### 2.5 C5 — from-scratch twins
   223	
   224	Required only where S itself trains from scratch (H-GU); recipe and pins are
   225	P3-D-BASE/P3-LR-TINY property (isoFLOP placement, same-data-same-order, ≥3 paired
   226	seeds); the builder only ADMITS the twin into F(B_k) and measures it under the
   227	common ledger [STIPULATED: P3F-A2].
   228	
   229	### 2.6 Composition rule
   230	
   231	Transforms compose along a pinned lattice: {C1 donor} × {quant level} × {± C3
   232	retrieval} × {± C4 TTC policy}; pruning/distillation entries compose with quant
   233	only. The FULL composed grid at R-1 is enumerable (≈ low hundreds of static
   234	candidates before byte-filtering); §3's staged screen keeps measurement inside the
   235	free-compute pool [STIPULATED: P3F-A2].

   238	
   239	## 3. The builder procedure (six stages, executable)
   240	
   241	The pipeline below is the buildable artifact this bead specifies; P3-T-FRONT-IMPL
   242	(§13) implements it.
   243	
   244	```
   245	S0 ENUMERATE   roster × transform lattice → static candidate list
   246	               + compute packed bytes analytically → drop static-infeasible (LOGGED)
   247	S1 SCREEN      dev mini-suite (pinned ~10% stratified subset of the dev split)
   248	               → cheap dev-index proxy per candidate; successive halving keeps
   249	               top half per family, ≥2 rounds
   250	S2 OPTIMISE    survivors get metered tuning inside T_k (§3.3): decoding params,
   251	               retrieval k / thresholds, TTC policy params, recovery training
   252	               for pruned entries — FIXED selection rule (§3.2), all spend logged
   253	S3 MEASURE     full dev-split run under KOT-SIZE/2 + KOT-COST/2 on the P3-D-HW
   254	               rig, warm + cold, pinned concurrency + output-length discipline
   255	S4 ADMIT       filter against every component of B_k → F(B_k) candidate set
   256	S5 ENVELOPE    staircase + Pareto extraction over ALL measured points; dominated
   257	               + inadmissible points reported; B_k marked
   258	S6 PIN         freeze F(B_k): config/weights/index/verifier hashes, seeds,
   259	               decoding params, the full builder log → content hash → the
   260	               frontier version for that rung
   261	```
   262	
   263	### 3.1 Dev split (leakage discipline)
   264	
   265	- DECISION: all builder selection runs on a **dev split disjoint from the frozen
   266	  index suite AND from every sealed release by construction** (sealed items are
   267	  produced post-freeze and never touch the builder; ASM-0812). The dev split is
   268	  pinned by P3-D-INDEX alongside the suite; the builder never sees a frozen-suite
   269	  or sealed item [STIPULATED: ASM-0812 executed; P3F-A2].
   270	
   271	### 3.2 The fixed selection rule
   272	
   273	- DECISION: per candidate family, select the config maximising the **dev-split
   274	  estimate of KOT-AI-INDEX/2 restricted to that rung's above-floor components**
   275	  (the floor/exclusion criterion registered from P3-E-CAL calibration data, per
   276	  EVAL's correction — components may not be dropped post hoc), subject to
   277	  admissibility; ties broken by lower measured cost vector (lexicographic:
   278	  energy, then p95). Near-floor MC components are excluded from the SELECTION
   279	  metric because their signal is noise near chance [LIT-BACKED: EVAL.md §5
   280	  (Schaeffer arXiv:2304.15004, 2023)] — they remain in the frozen-suite
   281	  MEASUREMENT of the final comparators [STIPULATED: ASM-0812 fixed-selection-rule
   282	  clause executed; P3F-A2].
   283	
   284	### 3.3 Total-tuning-compute symmetry (the T_k meter)
   285	
   286	- DECISION: tuning symmetry binds on **total tuning compute actually spent**
   287	  (accelerator-hours + CPU-hours, logged per arm into KOT-LIFE/1), NOT config
   288	  counts (ASM-0812; data-matched ≠ compute-matched [LIT-BACKED: EVAL.md §7
   289	  (BabyLM-2 findings arXiv:2412.05149, 2024)]). Mechanism: a per-rung cap **T_k applies per arm** — every comparator
   290	  FAMILY (C1-quant, C2-pruned, C3-RAG, C4-TTC, C5-twin count as separate arms)
   291	  and the candidate system S each spend ≤ T_k on search + adaptation; the meter
   292	  is the builder's own job-level accounting, audit-trailed. Proposed values,
   293	  maintainer-adjustable at KOT-FAIR/2 freeze: T_1 = 25 GPU-h/arm (R-1), T_2 =
   294	  100 GPU-h/arm (R-2); R-0 arms run inside the free pool with the same logging
   295	  [STIPULATED: ASM-0812 tuning-symmetry operationalised; P3F-A3-T]. An S that
   296	  spends more than T_k has not won
   297	  (ASM-0812 verbatim); a comparator arm that exhausts T_k freezes at its best
   298	  logged config.
   299	- Donor pretraining compute is INHERITED and declared on the lifecycle ledger
   300	  (KOT-LIFE/1), never netted out and never counted against T_k — symmetric, since
   301	  S's families also start from donors [STIPULATED: P3F-A3-T].
   302	
   303	---
   304	
   305	## 4. The adaptive test-time-compute recipe (resolving RAG.md's five OPEN items)
   306	
   307	The lit-review verdict this section answers: "'implement Snell-style' is not yet
   308	an executable baseline recipe" [LIT-BACKED: RAG.md §4/§7.6/§8]. The verified
   309	envelope it must respect: the >4× and 14× findings are separate, conditional,
   310	PaLM-2/MATH-scale results with the verifier's training compute off their ledger;
   311	nothing is measured in our band or off math [LIT-BACKED: RAG.md §4 + EVAL.md §7].
   312	Clause (iv) accordingly reads "mandatory to CONSIDER, include where
   313	task-appropriate and budget-feasible" [LIT-BACKED: EVAL.md §9].
   314	
   315	1. **Budgeted difficulty estimator [STIPULATED: P3F-A3].** Default estimator:
   316	   **self-charging agreement escalation** — draw n₀ = 4 samples; if the majority
   317	   answer's agreement ≥ a pinned threshold τ, stop and emit it; else escalate to
   318	   the next stage of the policy ladder. The estimator IS the first stage of the
   319	   policy, so its compute is charged to the arm's per-query cost by construction
   320	   — there is no free oracle difficulty label. A trained router (small logistic
   321	   head on sample-agreement + logprob features) is an OPTIONAL grid member, its
   322	   training charged to T_k and its weights to bytes_max.
   323	2. **Eligible policy ladder per budget rung [STIPULATED: P3F-A3].**
   324	
   325	   | Stage | Policy | Enters the grid iff |
   326	   |---|---|---|
   327	   | P0 | greedy / single sample | always |
   328	   | P1 | self-consistency@N, N ∈ {4, 16, 64} adaptive | per-query cost fits B_k latency/energy |
   329	   | P2 | best-of-N + OUTCOME verifier (Cobbe-style) | verifier trains within T_k AND verifier bytes fit bytes_max |
   330	   | P3 | PRM-weighted BoN / beam (Math-Shepherd-style auto-labels, no human-label budget) | as P2; PRM auto-labelling compute also inside T_k |
   331	
   332	   Off-the-shelf open PRMs (e.g. the Skywork-PRM-1.5B class f2b already ran
   333	   [MEASURED: registry/verdicts/f2b-replicate.json comparator]) are eligible
   334	   ONLY where their bytes fit bytes_max — at the R-1 anchor budget
   335	   (135M-class bytes) a 1.5B PRM is inadmissible by arithmetic; the R-1 P2/P3
   336	   verifier is necessarily small and trained within T_k [STIPULATED: P3F-A3].
   337	3. **Verifier build costs on the ledger [STIPULATED: P3F-A3].** Verifier/PRM
   338	   training + auto-labelling compute → KOT-LIFE/1 (the ledger's cautionary-tale
   339	   clause [LIT-BACKED: EVAL.md §7]); verifier weights → KOT-SIZE/2 figure (1);
   340	   verifier inference → KOT-COST/2 per query. No component is free.
   341	4. **Base-success ≈ 0 fallback [STIPULATED: P3F-A3].** If stage-1 agreement is
   342	   below a pinned floor τ₀ (no two samples agree at n₀ AND max logprob below a
   343	   pinned percentile), the policy falls back to P0 (emit best single sample or
   344	   the benchmark's abstention token where the index scores abstention) and spends
   345	   nothing further — encoding the measured edge that test-time compute does not
   346	   rescue items the base model cannot touch [LIT-BACKED: RAG.md §4, Snell
   347	   envelope].
   348	5. **Non-math calibration gate [STIPULATED: P3F-A3].** Before any TTC arm
   349	   enters a frozen F(B_k): a calibration measurement (co-scheduled with
   350	   P3-E-CAL, same pinned harness) runs the policy ladder vs P0 on the DEV split
   351	   per index DOMAIN. A TTC policy enters the frontier only in domains where its
   352	   dev-split gain over P0 has LCB95 > 0 at that rung; elsewhere the arm freezes
   353	   as P0. The Snell >4×/14× figures are never assumed to transfer — transfer to
   354	   our band and suite is measured or the arm stays out
   355	   [EXTRAPOLATION-barrier made explicit; LIT-BACKED: RAG.md §7.6].
   356	6. **Large-budget decay reporting [STIPULATED: P3F-A3].** For every P2/P3 arm
   357	   the builder publishes the full N-sweep curve (N ∈ {1, 4, 16, 64} within
   358	   budget). If verifier-guided performance declines at high N (the scaling-flaws
   359	   shape — shown for the tested verifiers, not universal [LIT-BACKED: RAG.md
   360	   §4]), the frozen config takes the arg-max N (the selection rule already does
   361	   this) and the decay curve is published beside the frontier. Symmetric honesty
   362	   clause: the symbolic verifier's claimed immunity to this decay on its covered
   363	   slice is a claim P3-E-VL-1 must measure, never a premise here [LIT-BACKED:
   364	   RAG.md §5].
   365	
   366	---
   367	
   368	## 5. The matched-retrieval rules (resolving RAG.md §6's five OPEN rules)
   369	
   370	These five rules had "no published precedent" and were left open by the lit
   371	review [LIT-BACKED: RAG.md §7.1]. They are resolved here as **binding defaults**
   372	because the frontier's own matched-corpus arm (§2.3a) cannot be built without
   373	them; **P3-D-RAGC inherits them verbatim and instantiates the factorial cells on
   374	top; it may refine a rule only by registered amendment, never silently**
   375	[STIPULATED: P3F-A4].
   376	
   377	**Rule 1 — source-content derivation + information-parity criterion.** One pinned
   378	source snapshot (content hash) per vertical/corpus. Each arm's indexed objects are
   379	derived by a **pinned, automated, seed-pinned derivation script** (kernel records
   380	via the standing mint pipeline version; passages via a pinned chunker
   381	(size/overlap pinned); triples via a pinned extractor; tool schemas via a pinned
   382	generator) — human curation inside a derivation is permitted only if its hours are
   383	logged to KOT-LIFE/1 and disclosed. Parity is enforced as four MEASURED checks,
   384	and no further: (a) **source parity** — every arm's derivation consumes the
   385	identical snapshot, no arm-specific extra sources; (b) **byte cap parity** — every
   386	arm's packed indexed-object bytes ≤ the same store-byte allocation within B_k
   387	(equal cap, not forced-equal usage); (c) **screen parity** — the §2.2
   388	decontamination screen applies to every arm's derived objects identically;
   389	(d) **effort disclosure** — authoring/curation/build compute per arm co-reported
   390	(no fudged exchange rate, consistent with KOT-LIFE/1's no-common-unit rule).
   391	Residual honesty line, carried verbatim on every consuming claim: *information
   392	parity beyond these four checks cannot be certified — converting one snapshot
   393	into different object types changes the retrieval problem itself; this is a
   394	standing limitation, not a solved problem* [STIPULATED: P3F-A4; the RAG.md §6.1
   395	scope finding adopted].
   396	
   397	**Rule 2 — identical vs architecture-specific retrievers.** Two mandatory cells,
   398	each with a distinct claim licence: the **NATIVE cell** — each arm retrieves the
   399	way its architecture natively does (kernel addressing for S; BM25/dense for RAG)
   400	under the same resource budgets — is what enters **F(B_k)** (the frontier is
   401	"the best each architecture can do at the budget"); the **SHARED cell** — one
   402	pinned retriever+index over a common text serialisation of every arm's objects —
   403	is an **attribution instrument only** (isolates content representation at fixed
   404	retrieval; never a frontier member, because it structurally handicaps arms whose
   405	native retrieval is not dense-over-text). Every consuming prereg names which cell
   406	licenses which sentence [STIPULATED: P3F-A4].
   407	
   408	**Rule 3 — retrieved-token/context budgets + ordering parity.** Pinned per rung:
   409	max retrieved tokens per query (identical across arms), max context length,
   410	context assembly order = retrieval-score-descending (no arm may hand-place gold
   411	material at context edges — the position confound), and a position-shuffle
   412	control cell lives in RAGC's factorial [LIT-BACKED: RAG.md §6.4,
   413	Lost-in-the-Middle; STIPULATED: P3F-A4].
   414	
   415	**Rule 4 — generator / executor / retry / tuning parity.** Where the comparison
   416	is store-vs-store (matched-corpus arm vs S), the generator checkpoint, decoding
   417	parameters, retry budget (same max-k), abstention accounting, and tool-executor
   418	semantics are IDENTICAL across arms; tuning ≤ T_k per arm (§3.3). Where the
   419	comparison is whole-system frontier (free-corpus arm), the generator may differ —
   420	that arm's licence is "a better baseline exists at this budget", not attribution
   421	[STIPULATED: P3F-A4].
   422	
   423	**Rule 5 — construction leakage + authoring effort.** Every construction pass
   424	(embedding sweeps, LLM extraction, selector training — the TinyAgent DeBERTa
   425	lesson [LIT-BACKED: RAG.md §1]) is booked as build compute in KOT-LIFE/1 and its
   426	artifacts' bytes in KOT-SIZE/2 figures (1)/(5) on EVERY arm identically.
   427	Order-of-operations rule: derivation/construction pipelines are frozen
   428	(content-hashed) BEFORE any frozen-suite item is drawn; the §2.2 decontamination
   429	screen applies to construction INPUTS as well as outputs; a store or index whose
   430	construction post-dates suite exposure is VOID for W1 use [STIPULATED: P3F-A4].
   431	
   432	---
   433	
   434	## 6. NL-boundary discipline (no smuggled oracle)
   435	
   436	- DECISION: every frontier comparator consumes **natural benchmark text** — the
   437	  NL-input integrity rule (ASM-0808) applies to comparators exactly as to S.
   438	  Pure-neural and RAG arms satisfy this natively: their NL handling is part of
   439	  their measured product, exactly as S's parser is part of S's
   440	  [STIPULATED: ASM-0808 applied to comparators; P3F-A2].
   441	- DECISION: the frontier NEVER receives gold parses, gold subgraphs, gold record
   442	  addresses, or gold passages. One labelled exception class exists for
   443	  DECOMPOSITION only: an `oracle-diagnostic` comparator (e.g. gold-passage
   444	  injection as a retrieval ceiling) may be built for a G2/G3 analysis, is
   445	  labelled per ASM-0814, **never enters F(B_k)**, and licenses no W1 claim
   446	  [STIPULATED: ASM-0814 applied; P3F-A2].
   447	- Asymmetry, stated so nobody "fixes" it later: the NLB gate (P3-E-NLB-1) binds
   448	  KERNEL-store addressing from natural input — a measured wall of the kernel
   449	  front-end [MEASURED: l3a-parse/a5-nl FAILs] — and does NOT bind the frontier's
   450	  baselines. Weakening the frontier to share the kernel's wall would fake
   451	  exactly the win W1 exists to make honest [STIPULATED: P3F-A2].
   452

133	## 0. What Programme-3 is, and what it inherits
   134	
   135	The maintainer's intent, restated: use something *like* the kernel + world
   136	information this programme has built to create **neurosymbolic AI architectures**
   137	that (1) achieve a **higher AI index than comparably-resourced models**, and then
   138	(2) find how **small** the whole system (model + kernel + world + everything else)
   139	can be made while staying competitive on that index. Because the system is
   140	neurosymbolic, size cannot be parameters: size is **deployment bytes** plus a full
   141	**inference resource vector**. Start very small to learn cheaply which
   142	architectures work; then continuously scale while improving them. (The review is
   143	right that the literal "higher than ALL other models of the same size and compute"
   144	is not a testable claim; §1.4 narrows what W1 actually licenses — the maintainer's
   145	north-star is preserved as the *aspiration*, the testable claim is the frontier
   146	statement.)
   147	
   148	Programme-3 is a **new goal**, not a new evidence base. It inherits Programme-1/2's
   149	instruments, seams, and — critically — its measured walls. The honest starting
   150	position, restated from the capstone with its own tags:
   151	
   152	- PREMISE: across the entire registry there are ZERO audited end-task wins over the
   153	  kernel-as-text null attributable to the kernel's content; both value theses stand

   160	- PREMISE: both measured natural-language crossings FAILED — l3a-parse retains

   220	## 1. North-star and falsifiable success criteria
   315	### 1.3 Inference cost — KOT-COST/2 (the resource vector) + the neural-FLOP diagnostic
   316	
   317	The review's technical error #7 is accepted: a ledger in which symbolic
   318	computation enters at zero is not a compute measure — a system doing substantial
   319	CPU search, retrieval, decompression, or I/O could look "compute-matched" by
   320	definition. Revision 1's L1 is therefore RENAMED and DEMOTED to what it actually
   321	is, and the binding ledger becomes a full resource vector [STIPULATED: ASM-0810].
   322	
   323	- DECISION: inference cost is the measured **resource vector KOT-COST/2**, per
   324	  query and per suite, on pinned hardware under the P3-D-HW repeatability
   325	  protocol: (a) accelerator time + estimated accelerator ops; (b) CPU-seconds;
   326	  (c) bytes read from storage and network; (d) energy (RAPL on CPU,
   327	  NVML/`nvidia-smi`-class counters on GPU, whole-run integration — not query-level
   328	  spot reads); (e) peak accelerator memory AND host RSS; (f) end-to-end latency at
   329	  p50 and p95; (g) throughput under a pinned concurrency distribution; (h) warm-
   330	  and cold-cache conditions both measured, including startup/index-load time;
   331	  (i) time-to-first-token and inter-token latency for generative arms; (j) output
   332	  length controlled (pinned max-token + stop discipline) or cost normalised per
   333	  emitted token, pinned per benchmark. Methodology borrows MLPerf inference +
   334	  power measurement discipline (REVIEW-CITED: MLPerf inference-datacenter
   335	  benchmarks + MLPerf power methodology; verified and operationalised by
   336	  P3-LR-SYS → P3-D-HW before the framework freezes) [STIPULATED: ASM-0810].
   337	- DECISION: the analytic neural-FLOP ledger survives as the **neural-FLOP
   338	  diagnostic** (2·params·tokens + attention terms, pinned formula sheet): useful
   339	  to keep the neural parts honest and hardware-independent, REPORTED always,
   340	  BINDING never — no "compute-matched" claim may cite it alone [STIPULATED:
   341	  ASM-0810; corrects technical error #7].
   342	
   343	Precedent that the symbolic side can be genuinely cheap on the measured vector —
   344	and that the accounting has to be measured, not assumed:
   345	
   346	- PREMISE: the deterministic engine answers covered queries at 5.29–7.82 µs/query,
   347	  and the f2b system-level cost ratio vs the 1.7B host was measured at 0.103
   348	  [MEASURED: registry/verdicts/l3a.json + registry/verdicts/a5.json engine timing;
   349	  registry/verdicts/f2b-replicate.json cost_ratio_vs_R3].
   350	
   351	### 1.3a The lifecycle ledger — KOT-LIFE/1
   352	
   353	The comparison this programme runs is a *deployment-efficiency* comparison, not an
   354	equal-total-investment comparison — legitimate, but it must be named accurately
   355	and priced [STIPULATED: ASM-0810, answering review "add a lifecycle ledger"].
   356	
   357	- DECISION: every W1-relevant system (S and every comparator) publishes a
   358	  **lifecycle ledger**: (1) donor pretraining provenance and compute where known
   359	  (dimension-drop arms *inherit* their donor's training compute — stated, never
   360	  netted out); (2) architecture fine-tuning + hyperparameter-search compute
   361	  actually spent (the tuning-symmetry audit trail, §2.3); (3) store costs:
   362	  authoring, parsing, embedding, indexing, and human review, in hours and $;
   363	  (4) total cost of ownership amortised at three pinned deployment volumes —
   364	  10^3, 10^6, 10^9 queries — using the measured KOT-COST/2 energy/latency figures.
   365	  Training longer to obtain a smaller model is a real deployment strategy and
   366	  optimal size depends on inference volume (REVIEW-CITED: Sardana et al., "Beyond
   367	  Chinchilla-Optimal"; verified by P3-LR-SYS). The ledger CO-REPORTS with every W1
   368	  claim and feeds kill condition K-P3v2(6); it does not gate W1 numerically
   369	  because store-authoring hours and GPU-hours have no non-arbitrary common unit —
   370	  the honesty mechanism is mandatory publication + the kill condition, not a
   371	  fudged exchange rate [STIPULATED: ASM-0810].
   372	
   373	### 1.4 The exact win condition and the exact shrink objective
   374	
   375	The revision-1 ±10% band construction is WITHDRAWN as gameable (padding into a
   376	favourable band; dodging a strong model just outside it; empty band
   377	intersections; losing to a smaller/cheaper dominator while "winning the
   378	neighbourhood") [STIPULATED: ASM-0810, accepting review ranked concern #1 in
   379	full].
