   437	## 2. The measurement + fair-comparison framework (where the programme lives or dies)
   438	
   439	Comparing a neurosymbolic system against the neural/RAG frontier at a fixed
   440	resource budget is the single hardest methodological problem here — harder than
   441	any individual architecture. The framework is frozen as **P3-MF (KOT-FAIR/2)**
   442	before any Phase-2 W1-relevant experiment freezes, and it now begins with an
   443	explicit threat model.
   444	
   445	### 2.0 The threat model (new; P3-D-THREAT)
   446	
   447	- DECISION: KOT-FAIR/2 includes a formal **resource/baseline threat model**: an
   448	  enumerated adversary analysis of how each metric can be gamed by either side —
   449	  packaging games (padding, base-image migration, remote dependencies), band/
   450	  frontier games (comparator dodging, budget shopping), cost games (warm-cache-only
   451	  reporting, output-length inflation/deflation, batch-size shopping,
   452	  symbolic-work-hiding), index games (suite shopping, abstention gaming, domain
   453	  reweighting), store games (answer-key encoding, error-analysis-driven authoring,
   454	  researcher adaptation to the frozen suite), and tuning games (unequal search
   455	  compute behind equal config counts) — each with its named mechanised counter in
   456	  this section. The threat model is a frozen artifact; a game not counterable at
   457	  freeze time is documented as a standing limitation on every claim it touches
   458	  [STIPULATED: ASM-0812; answers review §4 "formal resource/baseline threat
   459	  model"].
   460	
   461	### 2.1 Confound A — size/packaging asymmetry
   462	
   463	Answered by KOT-SIZE/2 (§1.2): canonical packing script, six co-reported figures,
   464	frozen base image, remote-dependency rule, artifact-freeze (store hash pinned at
   465	eval time, so "grow the store after measuring" is impossible by construction), and
   466	peak-RSS/cold-start measurement so lazily-paged stores pay their true cost on the
   467	resource vector.
   468	
   469	### 2.2 Confound B — the answer-key problem (the deepest one)
   470	
   471	A store can encode benchmark answers far more directly than weights can. The f2b
   472	lesson is exactly this in miniature:
   473	
   474	- PREMISE: the f2b verifier accepts iff the answer string-equals the canonical
   475	  record while gold is DEFINED by that same equality, so the measured +0.1507 is
   476	  provably consistent with "aligned answer key + retry", and the shuffled control
   477	  cannot discriminate content from alignment [MEASURED:
   478	  registry/assessments/f2b-replicate.json does_not_license].
   479	
   480	Mechanised answers, all mandatory for any W1 claim [STIPULATED: ASM-0812]:
   481	
   482	1. **Decontamination audit (hard gate, win-voiding):** automated n-gram overlap +
   483	   embedding-similarity screen of every kernel/world/store record against every
   484	   item of the frozen index suite, pinned threshold, human spot-check protocol on
   485	   flagged pairs; stores may not be authored, selected, or grown using any index
   486	   item, its paraphrase, or its source page. A win whose store fails the audit is
   487	   VOID, not caveated. (Neural baselines carry the standard training-data
   488	   contamination caveat symmetrically: we report known contamination status of
   489	   baseline corpora but cannot audit closed corpora — an asymmetry that favours
   490	   the baselines' scores, i.e. conservative for us.)
   491	2. **The factorial control design** (replacing revision 1's derangement-centric
   492	   list — derangement alone changes alignment and retrieval behaviour
   493	   simultaneously and cannot localise what mattered): controls span
   494	   **content-type × retrieval-architecture × executor**, drawn per experiment from:
   495	   (a) seed-pinned deranged store→item addressing (the f2b control, inherited);
   496	   (b) label permutation within records; (c) irrelevant-but-structurally-matched
   497	   records (same schema, types, sizes; unrelated content); (d) edge/relation
   498	   shuffling for graph arms; (e) the aligned-non-kernel store (matched-size,
   499	   matched-alignment, plain-English typed records — the knull lesson); (f) the
   500	   **matched generic-RAG/tool-use control** (§2.3, the strongest missing control);
   501	   (g) kernel-as-text null at matched token budget (the null Programme-1/2 never
   502	   lost to and that nsk1-g2d showed can be actively harmful — stays mandatory).
   503	   Each Phase-2 prereg names which cells it instantiates and what each
   504	   discriminates [STIPULATED: ASM-0812; answers review "Use a factorial control
   505	   design"].
   506	
   507	### 2.2a The sealed evaluation (new; P3-D-SEAL)
   508	
   509	Decontamination screening is necessary but CANNOT certify fairness: it misses
   510	benchmark-source knowledge encoded as rules/abstractions, selective store
   511	authoring guided by benchmark error analysis, paraphrases below threshold,
   512	contamination already in donor weights, and repeated researcher adaptation to the
   513	frozen suite (the review's list, adopted verbatim) [STIPULATED: ASM-0812].
   514	
   515	- DECISION: every W1 claim additionally requires a **sealed evaluation**: a
   516	  secondary suite produced AFTER architecture + store freeze, by an independent
   517	  party or a pinned procedural generator, unseen by every developing agent;
   518	  for store-based systems it includes temporally-fresh facts and/or independently
   519	  held-out domains. The sealed suite is versioned per release (LiveBench-style
   520	  refresh discipline — each release a new version, never silently folded in). Gate
   521	  shape: the sealed-suite result must be directionally consistent with the frozen
   522	  suite (pre-registered consistency band); a large frozen-vs-sealed gap voids the
   523	  win pending diagnosis [STIPULATED: ASM-0812].
   524	
   525	### 2.3 Confound C — baseline strength, the frontier-builder, and tuning symmetry
   526	
   527	- DECISION: the comparator set F(B_k) is built by a standing **neural
   528	  frontier-builder** (P3-D-FRONTIER): for each pre-registered budget B_k it
   529	  produces the strongest baselines that fit, drawing on (i) the strongest open
   530	  pure-neural models at or below budget (pinned name+revision at freeze);
   531	  (ii) budget-optimising transforms — quantisation, structured pruning,
   532	  distillation — applied best-effort to those models; (iii) **neural + retrieval:
   533	  a conventional RAG / tool-use architecture over the SAME corpus, SAME
   534	  retriever/index, SAME budgets as the kernel arm** (the strongest missing control
   535	  — the aligned-non-kernel store does NOT replace it); (iv) optimised/adaptive
   536	  test-time compute — best-of-k self-consistency alone is too weak; the builder
   537	  implements compute-optimal adaptive strategies (REVIEW-CITED: the ICLR-2025
   538	  test-time-compute result; verified by P3-LR-RAG/P3-LR-EVAL) and learned/neural
   539	  verifiers where budget permits; (v) training-compute-matched from-scratch twins,
   540	  required only where the neurosymbolic arm itself trains from scratch (H-GU)
   541	  [STIPULATED: ASM-0812].
   542	- DECISION: the six-way attribution factorisation is a standing analysis
   543	  requirement: any observed win must be decomposed as far as the control cells
   544	  allow across **kernel semantics vs structured storage vs retrieval vs
   545	  deterministic execution vs retry/search vs neural-symbolic integration** — the
   546	  factorial cells in §2.2(2) + the RAG control are exactly the instruments for
   547	  this [STIPULATED: ASM-0812].
   548	- DECISION: tuning symmetry binds on **total tuning compute** (accelerator-hours +
   549	  CPU-hours actually spent on search, logged per arm into KOT-LIFE/1), with a
   550	  FIXED, pre-registered selection rule on a dev split disjoint from the index
   551	  suite — equal config counts are NOT sufficient (configs differ enormously in
   552	  cost). An architecture that only wins with more search compute has not won
   553	  [STIPULATED: ASM-0812; corrects revision 1's config-count rule].
   554	
   555	### 2.4 What P3-MF-0 must deliver (the framework as an artifact)
   556	
   557	A frozen `KOT-FAIR/2` spec: the threat model (§2.0); index suite pins + domain
   558	structure + normalisation constants (§1.1); the KOT-SIZE/2 canonical packing
   559	script + six-figure recipe + frozen base image hash; the KOT-COST/2 measurement
   560	rig + the P3-D-HW hardware-repeatability and warm/cold protocol; the KOT-LIFE/1
   561	ledger template; the decontamination screener; the sealed-eval production
   562	protocol; the frontier-builder spec + comparator pinning rule; the statistical
   563	analysis plan (§2.5); and a **calibration report**: the full framework run on
   564	pure-neural models ONLY (135M vs 360M vs 1.7B), demonstrating that the index
   565	separates known-ordered models with the expected ordering and stable variance,
   566	and that the cost rig reproduces across days/reboots within its pre-registered
   567	repeatability band — the instrument is validated on cases with known answers
   568	before it ever judges a neurosymbolic system. Phase-0/1 work on the local box +
   569	free credits, ~$0.
   570	
   571	### 2.5 Statistical specification (corrected)
   572	
   573	- DECISION: the house statistical rules for Programme-3, correcting revision 1's
   574	  two errors [STIPULATED: ASM-0813; answers review "Statistical specification
   575	  needs correction" + technical errors #1/#2]:
   576	  1. **Margin wins:** a margin-δ superiority claim requires
   577	     `LCB95(INDEX(S) − INDEX(B)) > δ` — the lower confidence bound itself clears
   578	     the margin. A point estimate above δ with an LCB merely > 0 is NOT a margin
   579	     win and is never reported as one.
   580	  2. **Multiplicity:** S is tested against multiple comparators (and multiple
   581	     domains): use simultaneous confidence bounds / family-wise error control
   582	     across the pre-registered comparator set; the FWER procedure is pinned in the
   583	     analysis plan at freeze.
   584	  3. **Resampling:** hierarchical bootstrap across benchmark families and items,
   585	     preserving paired predictions (same items, per-system outputs paired).
   586	  4. **Non-inferiority vs equivalence:** a "retains capability" claim is
   587	     NON-INFERIORITY and uses a single one-sided test / one-sided CI against the
   588	     pre-registered non-inferiority margin. TOST (two one-sided tests) is an
   589	     EQUIVALENCE procedure and is reserved for genuine equivalence claims. All
   590	     revision-1 uses of "TOST" for retention claims (the H-DD central claim
   591	     included) are corrected accordingly.
   592	  5. One primary endpoint per experiment; kill criteria verbatim in the prereg;
   593	     verdicts generated by the mechanical grader and cross-vendor audited, per
   594	     standing practice.

854	## 4. The gate ladder (information-gaining gates × compute rungs)
   855	
   856	Revision 1 organised progression by compute rung alone, which the review
   857	correctly noted lets an architecture fail ambiguously (graph encoder? retrieval?
   858	NL parse?). Revision 2 organises every family's progression around five
   859	**information-gaining gates**, run cheapest-first, so a failure LOCALISES
   860	[STIPULATED: ASM-0817]:
   861	
   862	| Gate | Question | Input regime | Cost class | What a FAIL localises to |
   863	|---|---|---|---|---|
   864	| **G1 coverage-ceiling** | Can PERFECT oracle use of the store move the index by δ? (`Δ_max ≤ Σ_b w_b κ_b (1−a_b^cov)`) | paper/CPU: census + baseline accuracies | ~$0, no GPU | coverage — kill without touching the mechanism |
   865	| **G2 oracle-interface** | With gold parses/subgraphs/record addresses, does the mechanism help at all? | ORACLE inputs; labelled `oracle-diagnostic`; NO W1 claim (ASM-0814) | cheap GPU | the mechanism itself (encoder, fusion, placement) |
   866	| **G3 boundary-degradation** | How much of the G2 effect survives realistic NL mapping? | natural benchmark text through the NLB-gated front-end; the P3-D-ORACLE decomposition attributes the G2→G3 loss (parse vs retrieval vs execution vs generation) | cheap-moderate | the NL front-end / retrieval addressing |
   867	| **G4 full-system-vs-frontier** | Does the whole system beat the resource-constrained frontier F(B_k)? (= a W1 attempt) | natural inputs, full framework, sealed eval | the rung's main spend | the competitive thesis at that rung |
   868	| **G5 scaling** | Does the W1 margin hold or improve across ≥2 sizes? | as G4, two rungs | gated spend | scale-transfer |
   869	
   870	Compute rungs remain as HOMES for the gates (they say where the compute comes
   871	from, not what is learned):
   872	
   873	| Rung | Params-equivalent (orientation only; the binding constraint is the budget vector B_k) | Compute home | Est. cost |
   874	|---|---|---|---|
   875	| **R-0** | 1–30M, TinyStories-class data | Local box + single free GPU (Modal/ARC) | ~$0 beyond free pool |
   876	| **R-1** | 100–200M (SmolLM2-135M anchor) — the decisive cheap rung: every family's G1–G3 and first G4 | Oxford ARC / Modal academics; single-GPU jobs | ≤ ~50 GPU-h/family |
   877	| **R-2** | 300–500M (SmolLM2-360M anchor) — G4 for R-1 survivors; the W2 sweep iff W1 passes here | ARC/Modal; AIRR Gateway if awarded | ~100–500 GPU-h total |
   878	| **R-3** | 1–2B (SmolLM2-1.7B anchor) — G4/G5 at a size the outside world cares about; index version bump | AIRR Gateway 10k GPU-h class; TRC | gated: maintainer + secured allocation |
   879	| **R-4** | 3–8B | second-wave grants (§6) | gated: explicit maintainer fork |
   880	
   881	Rung/gate discipline: promotion requires the pre-registered primary of the
   882	current gate met; a family failing the SAME gate at two consecutive rungs (or
   883	failing G1/G2 outright, which are rung-independent) is closed at scope;
   884	architectures may change between rungs — every change re-enters through
   885	prereg-freeze; no silent drift. **Sequencing exemptions (per the review):**
   886	P3-E-NLB-1 and all coverage censuses/G1 computations do NOT wait for index
   887	calibration (P3-E-CAL) — they make no index claim and gate everything else, so
   888	they run immediately; every G4 (W1-relevant) experiment DOES require P3-E-CAL
   889	PASS plus the frozen KOT-FAIR/2 [STIPULATED: ASM-0817].

  1054	## 7. Honest risks and kill criteria
  1055	
  1056	### 7.1 Programme-level kill — K-P3v2 (quantitative, hardened)
  1057	
  1058	Revision 1's kill contained a subjective escape hatch ("analysts attribute the
  1059	misses to fundamentals") and a soft default (open-ended maintainer fork). Both
  1060	are replaced [STIPULATED: ASM-0818; answers review "The programme-level kill is
  1061	too soft"].
  1062	
  1063	- DECISION: **K-P3v2 (verbatim)** [STIPULATED: ASM-0818]. The competitiveness
  1064	  programme is KILLED — not forked, not re-scoped in place — if ANY of the
  1065	  following pre-registered quantitative conditions holds at its named checkpoint:
  1066	  1. **Coverage ceiling:** after the P3-D-POWER analysis (including any funded
  1067	     coverage-growth ingestion wave completed by the R-1 checkpoint), the
  1068	     perfect-oracle bound Δ_max < δ_1 for EVERY store-dependent family on the
  1069	     frozen R-1 suite — perfect use of the store cannot reach the margin, so no
  1070	     mechanism result can save it.
  1071	  2. **NL boundary:** P3-E-NLB-1 (and its redesigns) fails the 0.90 retention OR
  1072	     the S2 fail-closed safety gate on BOTH verticals after **N = 2 pre-registered
  1073	     redesign cycles** beyond the first attempt.
  1074	  3. **Frontier:** no family's G4 result exceeds the neural/RAG frontier F(B_k)
  1075	     at BOTH R-1 and R-2 (i.e. W1 fails everywhere through R-2 with the frontier
  1076	     comparators properly built).
  1077	  4. **Attribution collapse:** every measured family gain vanishes against the
  1078	     matched generic-store / generic-RAG controls (the win is retrieval + typed
  1079	     storage, available to any baseline, and is absorbed into the frontier).
  1080	  5. **Boundary non-survival:** every family's G2 (oracle-interface) gain fails
  1081	     to survive G3 (natural input) at the pre-registered retention margin, across
  1082	     the N = 2 NLB redesign cycles.
  1083	  6. **Lifecycle domination:** the KOT-LIFE/1 ledger shows S's total cost of
  1084	     ownership dominated by a frontier baseline at ALL THREE pinned amortisation
  1085	     volumes (10^3/10^6/10^9 queries) for every family that passed G4 — a
  1086	     "win" that no deployment volume can ever justify.
  1087	  **Default on kill:** the competitiveness programme TERMINATES; restarting
  1088	  requires a NEW proposal with new evidence or a new mechanism class — there is
  1089	  no open-ended maintainer fork. (The maintainer can of course overrule — but the
  1090	  DESIGN's default is termination, and an overrule is an explicit new decision on
  1091	  the record, not this document's escape hatch.) Surviving single mechanisms
  1092	  (e.g. a G2-positive fusion result) are handed back as SCOPED instruments with
  1093	  their envelopes, not as a continued competitiveness claim [STIPULATED:
  1094	  ASM-0818].
  1095	
  1096	### 7.2 Named risks, each tied to current evidence
  1097	
  1098	**R1 — the answer-key trap at programme scale (highest severity).** The whole
  1099	programme could "win" by encoding eval-adjacent content into stores. This is the
  1100	f2b confound (§2.2) writ large; the decontamination gate + factorial controls +
  1101	the SEALED post-freeze evaluation (§2.2a — new, because screening alone cannot
  1102	catch abstraction-level leakage or researcher adaptation) are the mitigation, and
  1103	the win-voiding rule is deliberately harsh.
  1104	
  1105	**R2 — the coverage wall may bind before any architecture question does.**
  1160	assume either Programme-1/2 thesis true. If the correctness thesis eventually
  1161	resolves NOT-FEASIBLE (semantics unsound), Programme-3's stores degrade to "typed
  1162	records + checker" — which W1 can still legitimately win with (§0 re-aiming),
  1163	honestly labelled by the aligned-non-kernel + generic-RAG controls. If the
  1164	efficiency de-confound (knull / human f2b-transfer) resolves against kernel
  1165	content, H-VL's prior weakens sharply — and K-P3v2(4) already treats
  1166	attribution-collapse as a kill input. The verdict-moving pending items of the old
  1167	programme (knull ablation, f2b-transfer Stage-1, g2) remain independently valuable
  1168	and none is superseded by this document.
  1169	
  1170	### 7.3 What this document does NOT claim (corrected)
  1171	
  1172	No architecture herein is claimed likely to work; three of the seven families
  1173	start from measured cautions and two from nothing. The claims this document
  1174	actually makes are: the objective is now falsifiably operationalised at a
  1175	resource frontier (§1); the comparison has been made **auditable in several named
  1176	respects** — decontamination-screened, control-factored, threat-modelled,
  1177	sealed-eval-checked, lifecycle-priced — but it has NOT been shown capable of
  1178	being made fully fair: training-data contamination of closed baseline corpora,
  1179	lifecycle-cost incommensurability, baseline-search completeness, and residual
  1180	packaging/frontier gaming remain UNRESOLVED and are carried as standing
  1181	limitations on every W1 claim (§2.0); the design space is enumerated with
  1182	per-family cheap falsifiers (§3); and the work decomposes into a beadable
  1183	lit-review-first hierarchy (§5). Everything else is a hypothesis with a
  1184	registered tag and a priced test.

373	### 1.4 The exact win condition and the exact shrink objective
   374	
   375	The revision-1 ±10% band construction is WITHDRAWN as gameable (padding into a
   376	favourable band; dodging a strong model just outside it; empty band
   377	intersections; losing to a smaller/cheaper dominator while "winning the
   378	neighbourhood") [STIPULATED: ASM-0810, accepting review ranked concern #1 in
   379	full].
   380	
   381	**W1 (the resource-frontier competitive claim), verbatim:** at rung R-k, with a
   382	pre-registered resource budget vector
   383	`B_k = (max canonically-packed deployment bytes; max p95 latency; max energy/query;
   384	max peak accelerator memory + host RSS)`, a Programme-3 system S beats the
   385	baseline frontier iff, under the frozen KOT-AI-INDEX/2 version for that rung,
   386	own-harness:
   387	
   388	1. **Admissibility:** S fits within EVERY component of B_k, measured under
   389	   KOT-SIZE/2 + KOT-COST/2, warm and cold conditions per the P3-D-HW protocol
   390	   (energy explicitly binds here — fixing revision 1's omission of energy from
   391	   W1 while KOT-COST claimed it); and
   392	2. **Frontier dominance:** for EVERY comparator C in the pre-registered comparator
   393	   set F(B_k) — every pre-registered, reproducible open comparator and baseline
   394	   family that fits within every component of B_k, explicitly INCLUDING systems
   395	   smaller or cheaper than S, each optimised for the budget by the frontier-builder
   396	   (§2.3) — the simultaneous one-sided 95% lower confidence bound satisfies
   397	   `LCB95(INDEX(S) − INDEX(C)) > δ_k`, with family-wise error control across the
   398	   comparator set and a hierarchical bootstrap over benchmark families and items
   399	   preserving paired predictions (§2.5); and
   400	3. the decontamination audit (§2.2) AND the sealed-evaluation consistency gate
   401	   (§2.2a) pass; and
   402	4. the factorial controls (§2.2) behave as pre-registered (derangement/permutation
   403	   destroys any store-content-attributed component; the aligned-non-kernel and
   404	   matched-generic-RAG results are REPORTED as the attribution split, whatever
   405	   they show); and
   406	5. the lifecycle ledger (§1.3a) is published alongside.
   407	
   408	Reporting: the full **staircase/Pareto envelope** of neural and neural-retrieval
   409	points across budgets (index vs bytes, index vs cost) is published with S plotted
   410	against it — W1 means S lies significantly above the envelope at its budget, not
   411	that it won a favourable neighbourhood.
   412	
   413	**Scope of the claim licensed by a W1 PASS, verbatim:** "S exceeds every
   414	pre-registered, reproducible open comparator and baseline family searched under
   415	budget B_k by ≥ δ_k on KOT-AI-INDEX/2-vN" — NEVER "higher than all other models of
   416	the same size and compute"; the literal universal is untestable and is hereby
   417	retired from every claim surface [STIPULATED: ASM-0810].
   418	
   419	**W2 (the shrink objective), verbatim:** once W1 first holds at any rung, minimise
   420	KOT-SIZE/2 figure-(1) bytes subject to `INDEX(S) ≥ INDEX(M_ref)` where M_ref is a
   421	named, pinned open reference model (initial M_ref = SmolLM2-1.7B own-harness;
   422	revisited by maintainer fork per index version). The deliverable is the measured
   423	**Pareto frontier over (bytes, resource vector, index)**; headline success = at
   424	least one point that strictly dominates every measurable open point at the small
   425	end. Consistency rule (fixing the review's W2 finding): the R-2 sweep inside
   426	P3-E-SCALE-2 runs ONLY conditional on W1 passing at R-2; any compression curve
   427	produced before a W1 PASS (e.g. H-DD dose-response curves) is labelled
   428	**DIAGNOSTIC**, never W2 [STIPULATED: ASM-0817].
   429	
   430	Falsifiability: W1 is falsified per-rung by failing its own margin against the
   431	frontier; the programme has quantitative kill conditions in §7. There is no
   432	"moral victory" clause: frontier parity is a FAIL of W1, however interesting the
   433	architecture.

   558	structure + normalisation constants (§1.1); the KOT-SIZE/2 canonical packing
   559	script + six-figure recipe + frozen base image hash; the KOT-COST/2 measurement
   560	rig + the P3-D-HW hardware-repeatability and warm/cold protocol; the KOT-LIFE/1

   570	
   571	### 2.5 Statistical specification (corrected)
   572	
   573	- DECISION: the house statistical rules for Programme-3, correcting revision 1's
   574	  two errors [STIPULATED: ASM-0813; answers review "Statistical specification
   575	  needs correction" + technical errors #1/#2]:
   576	  1. **Margin wins:** a margin-δ superiority claim requires
   577	     `LCB95(INDEX(S) − INDEX(B)) > δ` — the lower confidence bound itself clears
   578	     the margin. A point estimate above δ with an LCB merely > 0 is NOT a margin
   579	     win and is never reported as one.
   580	  2. **Multiplicity:** S is tested against multiple comparators (and multiple
   581	     domains): use simultaneous confidence bounds / family-wise error control
   582	     across the pre-registered comparator set; the FWER procedure is pinned in the
   583	     analysis plan at freeze.
   584	  3. **Resampling:** hierarchical bootstrap across benchmark families and items,
   585	     preserving paired predictions (same items, per-system outputs paired).
   586	  4. **Non-inferiority vs equivalence:** a "retains capability" claim is
   587	     NON-INFERIORITY and uses a single one-sided test / one-sided CI against the
   588	     pre-registered non-inferiority margin. TOST (two one-sided tests) is an
   589	     EQUIVALENCE procedure and is reserved for genuine equivalence claims. All
   590	     revision-1 uses of "TOST" for retention claims (the H-DD central claim
   591	     included) are corrected accordingly.
   592	  5. One primary endpoint per experiment; kill criteria verbatim in the prereg;
   593	     verdicts generated by the mechanical grader and cross-vendor audited, per
   594	     standing practice.

854	## 4. The gate ladder (information-gaining gates × compute rungs)
   855	
   856	Revision 1 organised progression by compute rung alone, which the review
   857	correctly noted lets an architecture fail ambiguously (graph encoder? retrieval?
   858	NL parse?). Revision 2 organises every family's progression around five
   859	**information-gaining gates**, run cheapest-first, so a failure LOCALISES
   860	[STIPULATED: ASM-0817]:
   861	
   862	| Gate | Question | Input regime | Cost class | What a FAIL localises to |
   863	|---|---|---|---|---|
   864	| **G1 coverage-ceiling** | Can PERFECT oracle use of the store move the index by δ? (`Δ_max ≤ Σ_b w_b κ_b (1−a_b^cov)`) | paper/CPU: census + baseline accuracies | ~$0, no GPU | coverage — kill without touching the mechanism |
   865	| **G2 oracle-interface** | With gold parses/subgraphs/record addresses, does the mechanism help at all? | ORACLE inputs; labelled `oracle-diagnostic`; NO W1 claim (ASM-0814) | cheap GPU | the mechanism itself (encoder, fusion, placement) |
   866	| **G3 boundary-degradation** | How much of the G2 effect survives realistic NL mapping? | natural benchmark text through the NLB-gated front-end; the P3-D-ORACLE decomposition attributes the G2→G3 loss (parse vs retrieval vs execution vs generation) | cheap-moderate | the NL front-end / retrieval addressing |
   867	| **G4 full-system-vs-frontier** | Does the whole system beat the resource-constrained frontier F(B_k)? (= a W1 attempt) | natural inputs, full framework, sealed eval | the rung's main spend | the competitive thesis at that rung |
   868	| **G5 scaling** | Does the W1 margin hold or improve across ≥2 sizes? | as G4, two rungs | gated spend | scale-transfer |

   871	from, not what is learned):
   872

   874	|---|---|---|---|
   875	| **R-0** | 1–30M, TinyStories-class data | Local box + single free GPU (Modal/ARC) | ~$0 beyond free pool |
   876	| **R-1** | 100–200M (SmolLM2-135M anchor) — the decisive cheap rung: every family's G1–G3 and first G4 | Oxford ARC / Modal academics; single-GPU jobs | ≤ ~50 GPU-h/family |
   877	| **R-2** | 300–500M (SmolLM2-360M anchor) — G4 for R-1 survivors; the W2 sweep iff W1 passes here | ARC/Modal; AIRR Gateway if awarded | ~100–500 GPU-h total |
   878	| **R-3** | 1–2B (SmolLM2-1.7B anchor) — G4/G5 at a size the outside world cares about; index version bump | AIRR Gateway 10k GPU-h class; TRC | gated: maintainer + secured allocation |

1014	## 6. Compute, budget, and infrastructure (cheap-first)
  1015	
  1016	- PREMISE: the free-compute pool documented for this programme spans Oxford ARC
  1017	  (free A100/H100/GH200, near-certain approval), AIRR Gateway (up to 10,000
  1018	  GPU-h), TPU Research Cloud, Modal for Academics (up to $10k serverless GPU),
  1019	  ~$1k-class inference-API credits, and an already-secured $100 Anyscale credit,
  1020	  with dollar ceilings explicitly not guarantees [LIT-BACKED:
  1021	  docs/next/free-compute-recon.md, consolidated 2026-07-10 — programme-internal
  1022	  recon, figures verified at their sources on that date].
  1023	
  1024	Allocation policy by rung (all spend above the free pool is maintainer-gated):
  1025	
  1026	- **Phase-0 + P3-MF-0 + P3-E-CAL:** local box (2 shared cores, `nice -n 10`,
  1027	  checkpointed — standing practice) + free API tiers. ~$0. Lit reviews are
  1028	  API/web-bound, not GPU-bound. G1 coverage computations are CPU-only.
  1029	- **R-0:** local box for symbolic side + evals; single free GPU (Modal/ARC) for
  1030	  the H-GU sweep. Inside the free pool.
  1031	- **R-1 (the decisive rung):** ARC + Modal academics; each family's falsifier is
  1032	  designed to fit ≤~50 GPU-h. The whole R-1 wave should fit inside ARC-free +
  1033	  Modal-credit capacity even if every family runs — and the G1/G2 gate structure
  1034	  means several families will be killed before spending their allocation.
  1035	- **Sealed eval:** the cheap default is a pinned procedural generator (local box);
  1036	  the independent-party variant costs coordination, not compute — P3-D-SEAL prices
  1037	  both.
  1038	- **R-2:** ARC/Modal continue; the AIRR Gateway application should be in flight
  1039	  during Phase-0 (its ~1-month award latency is the schedule-critical item, and
  1040	  applications cost nothing but form-filling).
  1041	- **R-3+:** hard-gated: requires a secured allocation (AIRR award or equivalent)
  1042	  AND a maintainer decision under K-P3v2's default (§7.1). No R-3 bead is created
  1043	  until both exist.
  1044	- **Operational discipline carried over:** Modal attached-run hygiene (explicit
  1045	  `modal app stop` after killing clients; nohup+setsid for long runs) per the
  1046	  standing programme memory; every run niced + checkpointed on the shared box.
  1047	
  1048	Failure mode priced in: if no external GPU materialises, G1 (CPU-only), P3-E-DD-0
  1049	probes, and the H-VL/H-PS R-1 falsifiers remain feasible on ARC-free alone; the
  1050	programme degrades gracefully to its cheapest decisive tests rather than stalling.
  1051

  1053	
  1054	## 7. Honest risks and kill criteria
  1055	
  1056	### 7.1 Programme-level kill — K-P3v2 (quantitative, hardened)
  1057	
  1058	Revision 1's kill contained a subjective escape hatch ("analysts attribute the
  1059	misses to fundamentals") and a soft default (open-ended maintainer fork). Both
  1060	are replaced [STIPULATED: ASM-0818; answers review "The programme-level kill is
  1061	too soft"].
  1062	
  1063	- DECISION: **K-P3v2 (verbatim)** [STIPULATED: ASM-0818]. The competitiveness
  1064	  programme is KILLED — not forked, not re-scoped in place — if ANY of the
  1065	  following pre-registered quantitative conditions holds at its named checkpoint:
  1066	  1. **Coverage ceiling:** after the P3-D-POWER analysis (including any funded
  1067	     coverage-growth ingestion wave completed by the R-1 checkpoint), the
  1068	     perfect-oracle bound Δ_max < δ_1 for EVERY store-dependent family on the
  1069	     frozen R-1 suite — perfect use of the store cannot reach the margin, so no
  1070	     mechanism result can save it.
  1071	  2. **NL boundary:** P3-E-NLB-1 (and its redesigns) fails the 0.90 retention OR
  1072	     the S2 fail-closed safety gate on BOTH verticals after **N = 2 pre-registered
  1073	     redesign cycles** beyond the first attempt.
  1074	  3. **Frontier:** no family's G4 result exceeds the neural/RAG frontier F(B_k)
  1075	     at BOTH R-1 and R-2 (i.e. W1 fails everywhere through R-2 with the frontier
  1076	     comparators properly built).
  1077	  4. **Attribution collapse:** every measured family gain vanishes against the
  1078	     matched generic-store / generic-RAG controls (the win is retrieval + typed
  1079	     storage, available to any baseline, and is absorbed into the frontier).
  1080	  5. **Boundary non-survival:** every family's G2 (oracle-interface) gain fails
  1081	     to survive G3 (natural input) at the pre-registered retention margin, across
  1082	     the N = 2 NLB redesign cycles.
  1083	  6. **Lifecycle domination:** the KOT-LIFE/1 ledger shows S's total cost of
  1084	     ownership dominated by a frontier baseline at ALL THREE pinned amortisation
  1085	     volumes (10^3/10^6/10^9 queries) for every family that passed G4 — a
  1086	     "win" that no deployment volume can ever justify.
  1087	  **Default on kill:** the competitiveness programme TERMINATES; restarting
  1088	  requires a NEW proposal with new evidence or a new mechanism class — there is
  1089	  no open-ended maintainer fork. (The maintainer can of course overrule — but the
  1090	  DESIGN's default is termination, and an overrule is an explicit new decision on
  1091	  the record, not this document's escape hatch.) Surviving single mechanisms
  1092	  (e.g. a G2-positive fusion result) are handed back as SCOPED instruments with
  1093	  their envelopes, not as a continued competitiveness claim [STIPULATED:
  1094	  ASM-0818].
  1095	
  1096	### 7.2 Named risks, each tied to current evidence
  1097	
  1098	**R1 — the answer-key trap at programme scale (highest severity).** The whole
  1099	programme could "win" by encoding eval-adjacent content into stores. This is the
  1100	f2b confound (§2.2) writ large; the decontamination gate + factorial controls +
  1101	the SEALED post-freeze evaluation (§2.2a — new, because screening alone cannot
  1102	catch abstraction-level leakage or researcher adaptation) are the mitigation, and
  1103	the win-voiding rule is deliberately harsh.
  1104	
  1105	**R2 — the coverage wall may bind before any architecture question does.**
  1160	assume either Programme-1/2 thesis true. If the correctness thesis eventually
  1161	resolves NOT-FEASIBLE (semantics unsound), Programme-3's stores degrade to "typed
  1162	records + checker" — which W1 can still legitimately win with (§0 re-aiming),
  1163	honestly labelled by the aligned-non-kernel + generic-RAG controls. If the
  1164	efficiency de-confound (knull / human f2b-transfer) resolves against kernel
  1165	content, H-VL's prior weakens sharply — and K-P3v2(4) already treats
  1166	attribution-collapse as a kill input. The verdict-moving pending items of the old
  1167	programme (knull ablation, f2b-transfer Stage-1, g2) remain independently valuable
  1168	and none is superseded by this document.
  1169	
  1170	### 7.3 What this document does NOT claim (corrected)
  1171	
  1172	No architecture herein is claimed likely to work; three of the seven families
  1173	start from measured cautions and two from nothing. The claims this document
  1174	actually makes are: the objective is now falsifiably operationalised at a
  1175	resource frontier (§1); the comparison has been made **auditable in several named
  1176	respects** — decontamination-screened, control-factored, threat-modelled,
  1177	sealed-eval-checked, lifecycle-priced — but it has NOT been shown capable of
  1178	being made fully fair: training-data contamination of closed baseline corpora,
  1179	lifecycle-cost incommensurability, baseline-search completeness, and residual
  1180	packaging/frontier gaming remain UNRESOLVED and are carried as standing
  1181	limitations on every W1 claim (§2.0); the design space is enumerated with
  1182	per-family cheap falsifiers (§3); and the work decomposes into a beadable
  1183	lit-review-first hierarchy (§5). Everything else is a hypothesis with a
  1184	registered tag and a priced test.
  1185	
  1186	---
  1187	
  1188	## Epistemic register (what this design relied on)

item.text: The design is much more concrete than a typical Phase-1 proposal, but two load-bearing mismatches are emerging: its “matched RAG” native cell relaxes the programme’s explicit same-retriever/index control, and its tuning/compute caps are not dimensionally closed. I’m checking these against the literature hand-offs and the KOT-FAIR/2 accounting language before ranking the verdict.

    52	## 1. Strongest small-model (100M–2B) RAG and tool-use recipes — and what they achieve under MATCHED accounting
    53	
    54	**State of the art, retrieval side.** The published wins that matter at or near our band:
    55	
    56	- **kNN-LM** (ICLR 2020, https://arxiv.org/abs/1911.00172): interpolating a Wikitext-103 LM with a
    57	  nearest-neighbour datastore over its own training set cuts perplexity 18.65 → **15.79 with no
    58	  additional training** [MEASURED]. The win is bought with a token-level datastore + ANN search at
    59	  every step — parameter count is flat but deployment bytes and query-time cost balloon (the paper
    60	  does not charge for either).
    61	- **RETRO** (arXiv 2021, ICML 2022, https://arxiv.org/abs/2112.04426): comparable performance to
    62	  GPT-3/Jurassic-1 on the Pile "despite using 25× fewer parameters" — conditioned on a **2-trillion-token
    63	  retrieval database** [MEASURED]. The 25× headline is a *parameter* claim, not a deployment-bytes
    64	  claim; a bytes-inclusive comparison has **not been performed** (by the authors or anyone found in
    65	  this sweep) — it plausibly reverses, but that is a calculation the P3-D-RAGC ledger must actually
    66	  run, not a published result [EXTRAPOLATION, unperformed calculation].
    67	- **Atlas** (JMLR 24(251), 2023, https://arxiv.org/abs/2208.03299, https://www.jmlr.org/papers/v24/23-0037.html):
    68	  64-shot NQ >42%, beating a 540B-parameter model by 3% with "50× fewer parameters" [MEASURED] —
    69	  again excluding the retrieval index (full Wikipedia + CC corpus) from the size claim.
    70	- **When retrieval actually pays:** Mallen et al. (ACL 2023, https://arxiv.org/abs/2212.10511) show on
    71	  PopQA (14k questions, 10 models) that "retrieval-augmented LMs largely outperform orders of
    72	  magnitude larger LMs" **specifically on long-tail entities**, that scaling parameters "fails to
    73	  appreciably improve memorization of factual knowledge in the long tail", and that *adaptive*
    74	  retrieval (retrieve only when needed, popularity-thresholded) improves accuracy while cutting
    75	  inference cost [MEASURED].
    76	- **Datastore scaling is a real axis:** MassiveDS (NeurIPS 2024,
    77	  https://proceedings.neurips.cc/paper_files/paper/2024/hash/a5d8aba27dfef4e849e8cb03fb87a954-Abstract-Conference.html,
    78	  https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12854): a 1.4T-token open datastore; datastore size improves LM and
    79	  downstream performance monotonically without obvious saturation, and **"a smaller model augmented
    80	  with a large datastore outperforms a larger LM-only model on knowledge-intensive tasks"**, with
    81	  compute-optimal curves showing larger datastores dominate at fixed *training* compute [MEASURED].
    82	- **Self-RAG** (arXiv 2023, ICLR 2024, https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.11511): 7B/13B LMs trained with
    83	  reflection tokens (learned when-to-retrieve + self-critique) outperform ChatGPT and
    84	  retrieval-augmented Llama2-chat on open-domain QA/fact-verification [MEASURED]. 7B is above our
    85	  band but the *recipe* (learned adaptive retrieval + critique) is budget-portable.
    86	
    87	**Tool-use side, at ≤2B genuinely:**
    88	
    89	- **xLAM-1B / APIGen** (arXiv 2024, https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.18518): a 1B model trained on 60k
    90	  execution-verified synthetic function-calling examples "surpass[es] GPT-3.5-Turbo and Claude-3
    91	  Haiku" on the Berkeley Function-Calling Benchmark (the 7B sibling beats several GPT-4 variants)
    92	  [MEASURED]. The lever is **verified synthetic data quality** (format check → actual execution →
    93	  semantic check), not architecture. Scope note: function-calling-specific, but broad within that —
    94	  the dataset spans **3,673 executable APIs across 21 categories** [MEASURED], so it is not
    95	  narrow-curated in the TinyAgent sense.
    96	- **TinyAgent-1.1B** (EMNLP 2024 demo, https://aclanthology.org/2024.emnlp-demo.9/,
    97	  https://arxiv.org/abs/2409.00608): fine-tuned on 40k curated use-cases + a tool-retrieval step
    98	  ("ToolRAG") + quantisation, reports 80.06% success vs GPT-4-Turbo's 79.08% **on its own curated
    99	  task suite** — a **16-tool, task-specific Mac-assistant benchmark** [MEASURED; numbers from the
   100	  project's paper/README — domain-narrow by construction]. Ledger note: ToolRAG uses a **separately
   101	  fine-tuned DeBERTa tool selector**; its weights, training and inference compute are part of the
   102	  system and must appear in any matched-resource ledger — the headline is accurate but not
   103	  resource-matched.
   104	- **Toolformer** (NeurIPS 2023, https://arxiv.org/abs/2302.04761): self-supervised API-call insertion
   105	  (calculator/QA/search/translation/calendar) yields zero-shot performance "often competitive with
   106	  much larger models" at 6.7B [MEASURED; above band, canonical recipe].
   107	
   108	**The decision-relevant synthesis** [EXTRAPOLATION, direction-only]: at 100M–2B, **this 28-source
   109	sweep found no published win over larger dense models under a deployment-bytes-inclusive,
   110	matched-total-resource ledger**. Caveat on scope: this was a targeted sweep, not a documented
   111	systematic search — read it as "none found here", not "none exists". Every "small beats large"
   112	retrieval result in the sweep charges parameters but not the index (kNN-LM, RETRO, Atlas, MassiveDS
   113	all follow this accounting); the ≤2B tool-use wins are domain-restricted, though not uniformly so —
   114	TinyAgent is task-specific by construction (16 tools, one assistant domain, plus a separately
   115	trained tool selector off-ledger), while xLAM/APIGen is function-calling-specific but spans 3,673
   116	APIs across 21 categories. This is *good news for the fairness case*: the
   117	matched-RAG control we must build is genuinely stronger accounting than the field's own norm, and
   118	the frontier F(B_k) at small budgets is likely to be **dense-model + adaptive test-time compute +
   119	modest local index**, not a trillion-token-datastore configuration (which busts any deployment-bytes
   120	budget we would set). It also means the G4 comparator recipes to implement are: (i) verified-synthetic-data
   121	fine-tune for tool use, (ii) adaptive/popularity-thresholded retrieval, (iii) datastore scaled *to
   122	the same byte budget as the kernel store*.

   126	## 2. Structured/KG retrieval vs dense retrieval — when each wins, and graph-size/depth dependence
   127	
   128	- **GraphRAG** (arXiv 2024, https://arxiv.org/abs/2404.16130): LLM-built entity graph + pregenerated
   129	  community summaries; wins over conventional RAG on **global sensemaking/query-focused summarisation**
   130	  ("main themes of the corpus" questions, ~1M-token corpora) on comprehensiveness/diversity —
   180	The depth-threshold framing above is a design *hypothesis* the sweep should straddle — not a prior
   181	it can assume.

   184	
   185	## 3. Honest index accounting — size, build cost, query cost
   186	
   187	What the strongest papers actually charge for, and don't [EXTRAPOLATION over §1–2 sources]:
   188	
   189	| Cost axis | Field practice | Honest instances |
   190	|---|---|---|
   191	| Index bytes | Almost never counted against the "fewer parameters" headline | RETRO states 2T tokens; MassiveDS states 1.4T tokens + releases the pipeline; kNN-LM's datastore is same-order as training set; MiniRAG reports storage (25% — author-reported, relative to its own LLM-RAG baseline) |
   192	| Build/construction | Rarely priced in full; GraphRAG-class methods add whole-corpus LLM passes (entity extraction + community summaries) — the flagship paper reports indexing runtime/wall time for its corpora but **no complete bytes/build-compute lifecycle ledger** | HippoRAG prices *online* retrieval (10–30× cheaper than IRCoT) but not offline graph build; GraphRAG-Bench evaluates the construction stage explicitly |
   193	| Query-time | Sometimes latency, rarely energy; kNN-LM pays ANN search per token (uncharged); rerankers/late-interaction win BEIR zero-shot **at heavy compute cost** (stated qualitatively) | BEIR (NeurIPS 2021 D&B, https://arxiv.org/abs/2104.08663) is unusually explicit that its best zero-shot systems are the computationally expensive ones [MEASURED] |
   194	| Comparability | Implementation variance so bad a toolkit exists to fix it | FlashRAG (arXiv 2405.13576; counts pinned to the **v2 abstract, revised 2025-02-24**): 16 reproduced methods + 38 standardised datasets (the 2024 v1 abstract reported 12 methods + 32 datasets — pin the release actually used when P3-D-RAGC adopts it), motivated by "absence of a standardized framework" making comparisons unreliable [MEASURED, v2 abstract re-fetched 2026-07-11] |
   195	
   196	**Decision take** [STIPULATED]: KOT-SIZE/2's six-figure recipe (canonical packed bytes, compressed
   197	distribution, warm RSS, cold working set, **index/store construction size and cost**, remote deps)
   198	is stricter than the accounting practised in **any of the 28 papers reviewed here** — a reading of
   199	this sweep, not a systematic-search result. Whether *any* published small-model RAG win would
   200	survive figure 5 as a "smaller system" claim is an **open calculation**: settling it would require
   201	a systematic search plus actual byte/build/query arithmetic per system, which neither this review
   202	nor anyone else has performed. P3-D-RAGC must therefore (a) charge the generic-RAG arm and
   203	the kernel arm identical ledgers (index bytes + build compute + query compute), and (b) pin the
   204	retriever/index implementation from a standard harness (FlashRAG-style) so the control is not
   205	weakened by our own implementation variance. The lifecycle ledger (KOT-LIFE/1) should book graph
   206	construction LLM passes exactly like store authoring — the literature's silent asymmetry
   207	(dense index ≈ free embed pass; graph index ≈ full LLM sweep) becomes an explicit line item.

   210	
   211	## 4. The ICLR-2025 adaptive test-time-compute result — VERIFIED, with envelope
   212	
   213	The review's citation resolves to **Snell, Lee, Xu & Kumar, "Scaling LLM Test-Time Compute Optimally
   214	Can be More Effective than Scaling Model Parameters for Reasoning", ICLR 2025 (oral)**
   215	(https://proceedings.iclr.cc/paper_files/paper/2025/hash/1b623663fd9b874366f3ce019fdfdd44-Abstract-Conference.html,
   216	fetched; also https://iclr.cc/virtual/2025/oral/31924). Its claims, restated inside their envelope:
   217	
   218	- **Compute-optimal (difficulty-adaptive) test-time scaling improves efficiency by >4× versus a
   219	  best-of-N baseline** — the strategy (best-of-N vs beam vs lookahead search against a PRM; vs
   220	  adaptive revision of the response distribution) must be *selected per prompt difficulty*
   221	  [MEASURED].
   222	- **In a FLOPs-matched evaluation, test-time compute lets a smaller base model outperform a 14×
   223	  larger model — on problems where the smaller base model has a non-trivial success rate**
   224	  [MEASURED]. On the hardest prompts (base success ≈ 0), extra inference compute does not rescue
   225	  the small model; pretraining scale still wins there. The exchange rate also depends on the
   226	  assumed inference-to-pretraining token ratio.
   227	
   228	Envelope constraints the headlines omit (all load-bearing for transfer to our setting):
   229	
   230	- The experiments used **PaLM-2-class models on MATH, with fine-tuned revision models and PRMs** —
   231	  not 135M–1.7B general-purpose models; nothing here is measured in our band or off math.
   232	- **Prompt-difficulty estimation itself consumes non-trivial test-time compute**; a faithful
   233	  implementation must budget the estimator, not treat difficulty as free oracle input.
   234	- The **>4× and 14× findings are separate comparisons** (strategy-selection efficiency vs a
   235	  FLOPs-matched model-size comparison) — they do not compose into a general "4× compute buys one
   236	  model class" exchange rate.
   237	- The authors **explicitly leave generalisation beyond math / easily-verified domains unresolved**.
   238	- The 14× result is **conditional on prompt difficulty and on the assumed lifetime
   239	  inference-to-pretraining token ratio**.
   240	
   241	Corroborating and bounding results:
   242	
   243	- **Inference Scaling Laws** (arXiv 2024, https://arxiv.org/abs/2408.00724): Llemma-7B + tree
   244	  search consistently beats Llemma-34B on MATH at equal compute; "smaller models combined with
   245	  advanced inference algorithms offer Pareto-optimal trade-offs" [MEASURED].
   246	- **Scaling Flaws of Verifier-Guided Search** (arXiv 2025, https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.00271): as
   247	  sample budget grows, verifier-guided search first beats then **underperforms plain repeated
   248	  sampling**, across models (Mistral-7B, DeepSeekMath-7B), benchmarks (GSM8K/MATH) and verifier
   249	  types — mechanism: **imperfect verifiers misrank and erroneously prune all valid paths**, worst
   250	  on hard/OOD problems [MEASURED]. Scope: this is evidence for the **particular learned
   251	  verifiers and search procedures tested** — it does not establish that every learned-verifier
   252	  frontier must degrade at large budgets.
   253	
   254	**What adaptive test-time compute actually buys** [EXTRAPOLATION, direction-only]: two *separate*
   255	results, not one exchange rate — (a) >4× efficiency over a best-of-N baseline from
   256	difficulty-adaptive strategy selection, and (b) in a different FLOPs-matched comparison, parity
   257	with a 14× larger model **on the mid-difficulty band** — with two hard edges: no rescue where base
   258	success ≈ 0, and (for the verifiers/search procedures tested in Scaling Flaws) decay at large
   259	budgets. The review is right: best-of-k self-consistency is too weak to serve as F(B_k)'s
   260	test-time-compute representative. But **"implement Snell-style" is not yet an executable baseline
   261	recipe**: P3-D-FRONTIER still has to design a *budgeted* difficulty estimator (its compute charged
   262	to the arm), the eligible search policies per budget rung, verifier training/build costs on the
   263	ledger, a fallback policy where estimated base success ≈ 0, and a **non-math calibration gate**
   264	before the >4× figure can be assumed to transfer (§7, §8). It must also report the large-budget
   265	regime honestly — where the tested learned-verifier baselines degrade, an effect our symbolic
   266	verifier is *claimed* to avoid on its covered slice. That immunity is a claim P3-E-VL-1 must
   267	measure end-to-end, not assume: the scaling-flaws result neither generalises to all learned
   268	verifiers nor establishes that a symbolic-verifier system is immune as a system.
   269	
   270	---
   271	
   272	## 5. Learned neural verifiers as baseline components (vs our symbolic verifier)
   309	## 6. Lessons on attributing a RAG "win" — separating retrieval from generation, corpus/index parity
   310	
   311	The methodological toolkit the field converged on, and the traps it documents:
   312	
   313	1. **Source-snapshot parity via a pinned shared snapshot.** KILT (NAACL 2021,
   314	   https://arxiv.org/abs/2009.02252) grounds all tasks in the *same Wikipedia snapshot* and scores
   315	   **provenance jointly with answers** [MEASURED] — the precedent for pinning the source snapshot
   316	   and for scoring *which record was used*, not just the answer. Precedent scope, stated honestly:
   317	   KILT does **not** mandate identical retrievers across systems, and it is **no precedent for the
   318	   cross-representation control P3-D-RAGC needs**. "Same source snapshot" is not "same corpus, same
   319	   retriever/index": converting the same source material into kernel records vs plain-text passages
   320	   vs triples vs tool schemas changes the *indexed objects* and the retrieval problem itself, and
   321	   the equivalence rules for that conversion have no published precedent (§ closing paragraph).
   322	2. **Always include the lexical baseline.** BEIR (NeurIPS 2021 D&B,
   323	   https://arxiv.org/abs/2104.08663): **BM25 is a robust zero-shot baseline** that dense retrievers
   324	   frequently fail to beat out-of-domain; expensive rerankers win. A "structured store beats dense
   325	   RAG" claim that never ran BM25 is unattributed [MEASURED].
   326	3. **Retrieval quality and generation quality move independently.** FiD (arXiv 2020, EACL 2021,
   327	   https://arxiv.org/abs/2007.01282): answer accuracy **scales with the number of retrieved passages
   328	   aggregated by the generator** — generation-side evidence aggregation, not retrieval precision,
   329	   drove SOTA [MEASURED]. Attribution requires reporting retrieval recall@k against gold provenance
   330	   *separately* from end-task accuracy.
   331	4. **Position confound.** Lost in the Middle (TACL 2023, https://arxiv.org/abs/2307.03172):
   332	   accuracy depends strongly on *where* in the context the gold passage sits (best at edges,
   333	   degraded mid-context) — context-order is a hidden variable in any RAG comparison [MEASURED].
   334	5. **The random-document trap.** The Power of Noise (SIGIR 2024, https://arxiv.org/abs/2401.14887):
   335	   high-scoring-but-irrelevant retrieved documents *hurt*, while **adding random documents improved
   336	   accuracy by up to 35%** in their setting [MEASURED, their envelope; mechanism contested]. A
   337	   measured "retrieval win" can be a prompt-perturbation effect — a random-document control arm is
   338	   cheap and kills this reading.
   339	6. **Gains concentrate in the long tail.** Mallen et al. (§1): retrieval's benefit is
   340	   popularity-stratified; unstratified averages hide whether the store or the parametric memory
   341	   answered [MEASURED].
   342	7. **Implementation variance is a first-order confound.** FlashRAG (§3): the motivation for the
   343	   toolkit is that un-standardised RAG comparisons were unreliable [MEASURED].
   344	
   345	**What this supplies for P3-D-RAGC — and what it must still design** [STIPULATED]: the literature
   346	supplies *necessary cells* for the control spec — same pinned **source snapshot** hash for kernel
   347	store and RAG index (KILT pattern, scope-limited per (1)); both BM25 and dense retriever cells;
   348	per-arm reporting of retrieval recall@k vs gold provenance separately from end-task accuracy (FiD
   349	lesson); fixed, pre-registered context ordering + a position-shuffle cell (Lost-in-the-Middle); a
   350	random-document cell (Power-of-Noise); popularity/long-tail stratified readout (Mallen);
   351	implementation from a pinned standard harness (FlashRAG-style). These slot directly into the
   352	§2.2(2) factorial as retrieval-architecture cells. **They are necessary, not sufficient: they do
   353	not yet constitute an executable matched control.** P3-D-RAGC must still resolve, from scratch and
   354	with no published precedent, at least these design rules:
   355	
   356	1. **Equivalent source-content derivation + an information-parity criterion** — a rule for
   357	   converting the same source snapshot into each arm's indexed objects (kernel records vs
   358	   plain-text passages vs triples vs tool schemas) such that no arm receives more or better-curated
   359	   information than another, and a way to *measure* that parity.
   360	2. **Identical vs architecture-specific retrievers** — when forcing one shared retriever/index is
   361	   the fair control and when it structurally handicaps an arm whose native retrieval operates over
   362	   different objects; the spec must decide per cell and justify it.
   363	3. **Retrieved-token/context budgets and ordering** — matched budgets for retrieved tokens and
   364	   context length, and an ordering-parity rule, across arms.
   365	4. **Generator, tool-executor, retry-policy, and tuning-compute parity** — same generator and
   366	   decoding, same executor and retry semantics, matched tuning/adaptation compute per arm.
   367	5. **Store/index-construction leakage and authoring effort** — construction passes (LLM sweeps,
   368	   human authoring, selector training) booked identically on both arms, with leakage of evaluation
   369	   knowledge into construction ruled out on both. On the six-way attribution
   370	factorisation (§2.3): the literature separates *retrieval* from *generation* well, separates
   371	*structured storage* from *retrieval* only recently (RAG-vs-GraphRAG unified protocol, GraphRAG-Bench
   372	stage-wise evaluation), and has **no precedent for isolating "kernel semantics" from "typed store"**
   373	— that cell (aligned-non-kernel store vs kernel store, same retriever) is our own instrument and
   374	must carry the load alone.
   375

   377	
   378	## 7. Open questions for Phase-1 (honest gaps this review could not close from the literature)
   379	
   380	1. **No executable matched-control precedent exists for cross-representation comparisons** — the
   381	   five design rules in §6 (source-content derivation + information parity; identical vs
   382	   architecture-specific retrievers; retrieved-token/context budgets + ordering; generator /
   383	   tool-executor / retry-policy / tuning-compute parity; construction leakage + authoring effort)
   384	   must be designed and pre-registered by P3-D-RAGC itself. The literature supplies cells, not the
   385	   control. [STIPULATED: this is the largest open item, and the reason P3-D-RAGC is *not* closed
   386	   by this review.]
   387	2. **No deployment-bytes-matched small-RAG-vs-dense frontier was found in this sweep** (not a
   388	   systematic search) — F(B_k) at our budgets must be constructed, not cited; the nearest recipes
   389	   are §1(i)–(iii). [EXTRAPOLATION]
   390	3. **No flagship graph-RAG paper publishes a complete bytes/build-compute lifecycle ledger**
   391	   (GraphRAG reports indexing runtime for its corpora; HippoRAG prices online retrieval only) —
   392	   P3-D-RAGC's graph cell needs its own measured build ledger; budget for measuring it, don't cite it.
   393	4. **Graph-size×depth break-even is unmeasured, and GNN-to-LM fusion has no source in this ledger**
   394	   — GraphRAG-Bench gives a difficulty gradient, not a size×depth sweep; the depth-threshold framing
   395	   in §2 is hypothesis, not prior. P3-E-GNN-1's sweep is novel and should be powered as such;
   396	   P3-D-GNN's empirical premise stays open pending P3-LR-FUSE and needs an explicit "no prior"
   397	   statement.
   398	5. **Verifier head-to-head (sound symbolic vs trained PRM, matched budgets, coverage-stratified)
   399	   has no precedent** — design from scratch in P3-D-FRONTIER/P3-E-VL-1. Neither the learned
   400	   verifier's large-budget decay (shown only for the tested verifiers/search procedures) nor the
   401	   symbolic system's end-to-end immunity is established — both are measurements to make.
   402	6. **Adaptive test-time compute at ≤2B on non-math tasks** is thin — Snell used PaLM-2-class models
   403	   on MATH with fine-tuned revisers/PRMs; Wu is Llemma/MATH; the transfer of the >4× efficiency
   404	   figure to our index suite at 135M–1.7B is an assumption until P3-E-CAL-adjacent calibration
   405	   measures it. "Snell-style" is also not yet a recipe: budgeted difficulty estimator, per-rung
   406	   search policies, verifier build costs, base-success≈0 fallback, and a non-math calibration gate
   407	   all remain to be designed (§4, §8). [EXTRAPOLATION]
   408	7. **Power-of-Noise mechanism** is unsettled in the literature — treat the random-document cell as
   409	   a control, not as a tuning opportunity.

320	  sealed endpoint is (S − B) ≥ δ_sealed with δ_sealed frozen in the prereg
   321	  BEFORE unsealing. Directional consistency with the frozen suite is necessary
   322	  but NOT sufficient: a frozen-suite win with weak or null sealed performance
   323	  fails the gate. The protocol must additionally register: (a) custody and
   324	  access rules for the sealed set; (b) ONE-SHOT use — each sealed release

   326	  disclosure of items and per-item outputs; (d) independent difficulty
   327	  calibration per domain (the GSM1k matching triple is the verified instrument
   328	  for that); (e) a no-feedback rule — anything learned from release N's
   329	  disclosure may only inform claims graded against a fresh release N+1
   330	  [STIPULATED].
   331	
   332	## 5. Proxy-rung validity: when a cheap rung predicts a dearer one
   333	
   334	The question P3-D-POWER and the G5 gate need answered: do R-0/R-1 measurements
   335	predict R-2+? The literature's answer is **metric-dependent**, in a way that
   336	directly shapes which metrics enter low rungs.
   337	
   338	- LOAD-BEARING: apparent emergence (sharp capability jumps that would make
   339	  small-scale results non-predictive) is largely an artifact of nonlinear/
   340	  discontinuous metrics (exact match, MC accuracy); under continuous metrics
   341	  the same models improve smoothly and predictably [LIT-BACKED: Schaeffer,
   342	  Miranda, Koyejo, arXiv:2304.15004, 2023 (NeurIPS 2023 — venue not restated
   343	  on the arXiv page)].
   344	- LOAD-BEARING: the mechanism for WHY downstream MC accuracy resists scaling
   345	  prediction: performance is computed from log-likelihoods through a chain of
   346	  transformations (normalize, compare against specific incorrect choices) that
   347	  progressively degrades the statistical relationship with scale — probability
   348	  mass on the INCORRECT options fluctuates unpredictably [LIT-BACKED:
   349	  Schaeffer, Schoelkopf, Biderman et al., arXiv:2406.04391, 2024].
   350	- LOAD-BEARING: nevertheless, aggregate capabilities ARE predictable
   351	  observationally: ~100 public models define a low-dimensional capability
   352	  space in which "emergent" phenomena, agent performance, and even the value
   353	  of post-training techniques (CoT, self-consistency) follow smooth sigmoids
   354	  predictable from smaller/weaker models [LIT-BACKED: Ruan, Maddison,
   355	  Hashimoto, arXiv:2405.10938, NeurIPS 2024 spotlight].
   356	- Corroborating instance: BBH tasks that looked flat ("emergent") under
   357	  direct prompting became smoothly improvable with CoT — the metric/method,
   358	  not the underlying capability, produced the cliff [LIT-BACKED: Suzgun et
   359	  al., arXiv:2210.09261, 2022].
   360	- Design consequences [STIPULATED]:
   361	  1. **R-0/R-1 components should be continuous or minimal-pair-logprob metrics**
   362	     (held-out LM loss; BLiMP/EWoK forced-choice logprob). Precisely stated
   363	     (the earlier draft's "carry predictive signal upward" was too strong): the
   364	     cited results show continuous metrics AVOID the metric-induced
   365	     discontinuity artifact and that some observational relationships are
   366	     predictable — they do NOT by themselves license cross-rung prediction for
   367	     BLiMP/EWoK, which must be validated empirically (the G5 two-size
   368	     confirmation IS that validation, not a formality). MC accuracy at a rung
   369	     where the model is near floor carries almost no predictive signal. This
   370	     supports (not "validates") rev-2's R-0 row (LM loss + BLiMP/EWoK) and the
   371	     MMLU-cloze drop [STIPULATED per review].
   372	  2. **Proxy-rung validity must be CLAIMED per metric family, not per suite**:
   373	     the G5 two-size confirmation should test the margin's direction on the
   374	     continuous metrics first, and treat cross-rung MC-accuracy extrapolation
   375	     as unsupported by default.
   376	  3. For P3-D-POWER: Δ_max computations at R-1 on near-floor MC benchmarks are
   377	     unstable (the baseline accuracy term a_b^cov is noise near chance). Two
   378	     corrections registered here [STIPULATED per review]: (a) the below-floor
   379	     EXCLUSION criterion must itself be registered from P3-E-CAL calibration
   380	     data BEFORE campaign results are seen — components may not be dropped
   381	     post hoc after looking at results; (b) the identity Δ_B = κ_B × Δ_covered
   382	     and its max-gain bound κ(1 − a_cov) are stated on the RAW-accuracy scale —
   383	     under chance/ceiling normalization the maximum covered-item contribution
   384	     must be divided by the relevant normalization denominator and carried
   385	     through the registered domain/benchmark weights, with uncertainty in κ and
   386	     in covered-subset accuracy propagated. P3-D-POWER must restate the bound
   387	     on the normalized scale before quoting any power number.

   422	## 7. What actually beat matched baselines (skeptical accounting)
   423	
   424	The review asked every lit-bead to judge wins under their accounting. In the
   425	evaluation-methodology literature itself the load-bearing "wins" are:
   426	
   427	- **Compute-optimal test-time scaling beat best-of-N and beat bigger models at
   428	  matched FLOPs**: >4× efficiency over best-of-N; a smaller model with
   429	  compute-optimal test-time strategies outperformed a 14× larger model in
   430	  FLOPs-matched evaluation [LIT-BACKED: Snell, Lee, Xu, Kumar, ICLR 2025 —
   431	  verified BOTH at proceedings.iclr.cc (the review's exact link resolves to
   432	  this paper) and arXiv:2408.03314]. Accounting: FLOPs-matched, but on
   433	  MATH-class tasks with a trained verifier/reviser — the "matched" side does
   434	  not include verifier training compute. The 14× result is also CONDITIONAL:
   435	  it holds where the smaller model already has non-trivial success on the
   436	  task, and depends strongly on prompt difficulty and verifier quality — it is
   437	  not a blanket small-model win [LIT-BACKED: arXiv:2408.03314]. This is the
   438	  strongest justification for the frontier-builder's clause (iv) — adaptive
   439	  test-time compute as a comparator capability that MUST BE CONSIDERED and
   440	  included where task-appropriate and budget-feasible (not unconditionally
   441	  mandatory for every frontier) — and simultaneously a lifecycle-ledger
   442	  cautionary tale (KOT-LIFE/1 must log the verifier's training cost on OUR
   443	  side too) [STIPULATED].
   444	- **GPT-BERT beat the BabyLM baselines at matched data budget** (10M/100M
   445	  words, same eval pipeline): BLiMP 81.2 vs 69.8 baseline at 10M [LIT-BACKED:
   446	  arXiv:2412.05149, CoNLL 2024]. Accounting: matched words, NOT matched
   447	  training FLOPs — the findings paper itself reports a strong FLOPs–performance
   448	  relationship across submissions, so part of the win is compute [LIT-BACKED:
   449	  same]. Lesson for P3-D-BASE: data-matched ≠ compute-matched; our
   450	  tuning-symmetry rule (total tuning compute) is the right axis.
   451	- **CLUTRR's GNN-over-symbolic-graphs beat BERT-over-text** on systematic
   452	  generalization and robustness [LIT-BACKED: arXiv:1908.06177, EMNLP 2019].
   453	  Accounting: the GNN receives ORACLE symbolic input — this is precisely an
   454	  `oracle-diagnostic` win in our terminology, not an NL-input win; it is the
   455	  2019 version of our own l3a/a5 lesson (exact machinery inside the wall,
   456	  boundary unpaid). It motivates the H-GNN family AND its ASM-0814 gating in
   457	  one datum [STIPULATED].
   458	- **Nothing in the composite-index literature shows a small model "beating"
   459	  matched baselines via indexing choices** — the reverse: metabench shows most
   460	  legacy-suite variance is one factor (§2), and GSM1k/rephrase results show
   461	  apparent wins evaporating on sealed replicas (§4). The methodological moral
   462	  for W1: index construction and sealing are where ILLUSORY wins are
   463	  manufactured or destroyed, which is why P3-D-INDEX/P3-D-SEAL gate every
   464	  W1-relevant experiment [STIPULATED].

   409	  backfill: the paper estimates maximum factual entropy stored per parameter

   411	  pays addressing, keys, schema and retrieval-index overhead, so "dropping N

   422	## 5. The H-DD bet's prior art: has anyone dropped semantically-identified parameters and backfilled from an external store?
   423	
   424	**Verdict: no exact prior art LOCATED IN THIS REVIEW** (session-bounded).
   425	After targeted search (WebSearch, 2026-07-11), no published work was found
   426	that (i) identifies concept-associated parameters/subspaces in an EXISTING
   427	pretrained model, (ii) removes them structurally, and (iii) restores the lost
   428	capability specifically from an external symbolic/typed store. The components
   429	each exist separately — which is both encouragement (each step has precedent)
   430	and the basis of the H-DD novelty claim [STIPULATED, search-bounded: absence
   431	of evidence after a bounded search, not proof of absence].
   432	AUDIT-TRAIL LIMITATION [STIPULATED]: the individual search strings,
   433	databases/engines, per-query dates and inclusion/exclusion decisions were not
   434	logged here or in `SURG.sources.jsonl`. This verdict therefore supports "no
   435	exact prior art located in this review", NOT a durable novelty finding;
   436	before the novelty claim becomes load-bearing, P3-D-DD (or the coordinator)
   437	should re-run the search under a logged protocol.
   438	
   439	Nearest neighbours, in decreasing proximity:
   440	
   441	1. **LMLM — train-time knowledge externalisation** (closest to the
   442	   *destination*): annotate the pretraining corpus with database lookups and
   443	   **mask retrieved factual spans from the loss**, so the model learns lookup
   444	   instead of memorisation; small LMLMs competitive with substantially larger
   445	   vanilla LLMs; facts editable in the DB [LIT-BACKED: arXiv:2505.15962,
   446	   2025]. Differences: trains a NEW model (no donor surgery, no identified
   447	   subspace); the store is a flat fact DB, not a typed kernel. H-DD's
   448	   distinctive claim survives; LMLM becomes both (a) evidence the
   449	   store-backfilled *end state* is reachable, and (b) a candidate comparator
   450	   arm at R-1 if training budget allows [STIPULATED]. Exact size-vs-match
   451	   magnitudes not extracted from abstract — UNVERIFIED pending full-text read.
   452	2. **Retrieval substitutes for parametric capacity**: RETRO matches
   453	   GPT-3/Jurassic-1 on the Pile with **25× fewer parameters** given a 2T-token
   454	   retrieval DB, and **RETROfit** retrofits a *pretrained* transformer with
   455	   retrieval cheaply [LIT-BACKED: arXiv:2112.04426, 2022]; Atlas: 11B
   456	   retrieval-augmented beats 540B PaLM on NQ-64-shot
   457	   [KB: kb/records/arxiv_2208.03299.json, not re-verified]. Retrofit =
   458	   add-capacity-without-removal; nobody measured *removal + retrieval
   459	   restoration* on the same model.
   460	3. **Factual capacity is separable and relocatable**: Memory Layers at Scale —
   461	   sparse trainable KV memory replacing FFN capacity beats dense models given
   462	   >2× compute and MoE at matched resources, largest gains on FACTUAL tasks,
   463	   scaled to 128B memory params [LIT-BACKED: arXiv:2412.09764, 2024]. The
   464	   architectural statement of H-DD's premise (facts live in a lookupable
   465	   structure, not dense compute) — from the *training* side.
   466	4. **Memorise-vs-retrieve is now a scaling-law question**: 30M–3B models under
   467	   fixed data budgets; retrieval helps at all scales; a 3-D scaling framework
   468	   (params × tokens × retrieval-corpus); optimal allocation task- and
   469	   saturation-dependent [LIT-BACKED: arXiv:2604.00715, 2026]. Use in
   470	   P3-D-DD's power analysis for how much store a given parameter cut plausibly
   471	   buys back.
   472	5. **Removal-without-backfill exists** (unlearning): SAE-subspace-guided
   473	   projection removes targeted knowledge selectively with utility retained
   474	   (SSPU) [LIT-BACKED: arXiv:2505.24428, 2025] — step (i)+(ii) without (iii).
   475	   And **interp-guided ablation-with-purpose exists** (SHIFT)
   476	   [LIT-BACKED: arXiv:2403.19647, 2025] — the causal-gate methodology.
   477	6. **Against**: the Hase localisation negative (§3) says the "identified"
   478	   step's evidence standard must be interventional; and the 2-bit/param result
   479	   (§4) says what's dropped is *capacity*, not neatly addressable facts —
   480	   both are why P3-E-DD-0 exists [LIT-BACKED: arXiv:2301.04213, 2023;
   481	   arXiv:2404.05405, 2024].
   482	
   483	---
   484	
   485	## 6. What actually beat matched baselines, under what accounting (the skeptical ledger)
   486	
   487	| Result | What it beat | Accounting honesty | Verdict for us |
   488	|---|---|---|---|
   489	| Minitron: pruned+KD beats from-scratch at same size, 40× fewer tokens [LIT-BACKED: 2407.14679] | from-scratch twin at equal size under ISO-COMPUTE, with UNEQUAL token counts (100B KD vs 150B scratch tokens — KD pays teacher forwards; §4.3/Table 11) | Donor pretraining compute EXCLUDED — legitimate for "derive a family from a paid-for donor", illegitimate for "cheaper than training" totals; tuning compute (teacher correction, importance search) also uncounted | A (iv)-comparator ingredient (§1 candidate); also the accounting template H-DD inherits (donor compute stated, never counted as free — programme §2.3 note "dimension-drop arms inherit their donor's training compute — stated") |
   490	| Sheared-LLaMA beats Pythia/TinyLlama-class at 1.3B/2.7B with "3% compute" [LIT-BACKED: 2310.06694] | open from-scratch models of equal size | Same exclusion; also comparators differ in data quality | Confirms direction at OUR scale (1.3–2.7B); margins there are the non-inferiority anchor |
   491	| SliceGPT 25%-cut ≈99% retention (70B) [LIT-BACKED: 2401.15024] | its own donor (no recovery training) | No-recovery, zero-shot; small-model figure is 90%, much worse | Never quote 70B retention for R-1 donors |
   492	| RETRO ≈ GPT-3 at 25× fewer params [LIT-BACKED: 2112.04426] | matched-token GPT baselines | Retrieval DB (2T tokens) and retrieval FLOPs/IO not in the param count — bytes accounting absent | Exactly why KOT-SIZE/2 counts the packed store; a W1-style claim needs the store on the scale |
   493	| Memory layers beat dense at >2× compute [LIT-BACKED: 2412.09764] | dense + MoE at matched resources | Compute-matched (good); memory params ARE counted | Cleanest matched-accounting win in the family |
   494	| Distillation beats supervised (regime-bounded) [LIT-BACKED: 2502.08606] | supervised student at equal student compute | Teacher cost handled explicitly (the point of the law) | Use the law to SET recovery budgets, not just cite it |
   495	| SHIFT improves generalisation by feature ablation [LIT-BACKED: 2403.19647] | unablated classifier + baselines | Human judgement in the loop (labelled) | Methodology import only |
   496	| SAE probing/downstream: negatives [LIT-BACKED: 2502.16681; GDM 2025] | vs dense/standard probes | Well-matched baselines — that's the finding | Grounds for probe-first ordering in §3 |
   497	
   498	Standing skepticism [STIPULATED]: every "X% of compute" claim in this
   499	literature excludes donor pretraining; every retrieval-parity claim
   500	under-counts store bytes/IO. KOT-SIZE/2 + KOT-LIFE/1 already close those two.
   501	A THIRD systematic bias is NOT closed by existing rules:
   502	**method-development/tuning compute** — teacher correction, importance
   503	search, dynamic-remix scheduling, recipe and hyperparameter selection — is
   504	invisible to an equal-recovery-tokens/FLOPs rule, so "equal recovery compute"
   505	alone does not make arms comparable. PROPOSED IN-DOC (for the coordinator; no
   506	registry write here): P3-D-DD's prereg must define a tuning-compute ledger
   507	that is symmetric across arms (every arm may spend it; all spending is
   508	counted). Additionally, quantization and the resource-frontier baseline sit
   509	outside this table's comparator and must be inherited from the P3-D-FRONTIER
   510	controls before any superiority claim. Existing accounting rules are
   511	necessary but NOT yet sufficient for the (iv) comparator.
   512	
   513	---
   514	
   515	## 7. Open questions for Phase-1 (P3-D-DD must answer or pin)
   516	
   517	1. **Donor pair + their derived invariance groups.** SmolLM2-135M (RMSNorm,
   518	   tied embeddings, RoPE) vs Pythia-160M (LayerNorm, parallel attn+MLP,
   519	   untied): derive (a) strict gauge group as-parameterised (expect B_d /
   520	   S_d-with-global-sign-flip per §2.3 — re-derive independently, the source
   521	   is a single-author preprint), (b) enlarged group after declared
   522	   refactorings (gain-fusing; LayerNorm conversion), (c) per-block Q_ℓ
   523	   schemes with explicit Qᵀ_{ℓ−1}Q_ℓ residual bridges (§2.1). Validation
   524	   standard: **end-to-end logits over a pinned corpus for every
   525	   transformation**, plus numerical-tolerance checks per site (incl. RoPE
